Notes: Baloch man fatally shot by Iranian government forces during a raid at around 10 p.m. He was the 14th Baluch civilian killed by direct fire from state forces since the beginning of 2026. Sources report that he had previously been sought by authorities in connection with a tribal dispute.

Executed by the Iranian regime after being sentenced to death for participating in protests on January 8, 2026 in Hamadan. He was arrested in February 2026 in Alborz Province and charged with 'Moharebeh' (Enmity against God) and the murder of Major Mahmoud Javad Bakhshian. The execution date and location were not disclosed in official announcements.
Arrested during the January protests in Iran. Their trial has been held on fabricated charges, and their forced confessions were broadcast on state TV. They are now at risk of heavy sentences.

Executed at Ghezel Hesar Prison in Karaj following confirmation of death sentence by Supreme Court. Arrested during January 2026 protests and charged with setting fire to the Jafari Mosque and the Imam Hadi Seminary in Tehran, participation in operational actions against national security to the benefit of Israel and the United States, destruction and arson of public and private property, clashing with agents, blocking streets, and obstructing traffic. Trial took place on February 21, 2026, at Branch 15 of the Tehran Revolutionary Court presided over by Judge Abolghasem Salavati. State media claimed he confessed to breaking down the door and setting fire to a mosque.
Arrested during the January protests in Iran. Their trial has been held on fabricated charges, and their forced confessions were broadcast on state TV. They are now at risk of heavy sentences.

Executed at Ghezel Hesar Prison in Karaj following confirmation of death sentence by Supreme Court. Arrested during January 2026 protests and charged with setting fire to the Jafari Mosque and the Imam Hadi Seminary in Tehran, participation in operational actions against national security to the benefit of Israel and the United States, destruction and arson of public and private property, clashing with agents, blocking streets, and obstructing traffic. Trial took place on February 21, 2026, at Branch 15 of the Tehran Revolutionary Court presided over by Judge Abolghasem Salavati. State media claimed he confessed to slogan-writing, blocking streets, and setting fire to motorcycles.
Was living in hiding after participating in Dey 1404 protests. Shot directly by IRGC forces in Qale-Kahvosh village, Dalaho county
After participating in Dey month 1404 protests, he lived secretly. Shot directly by IRGC forces in Qaleh-Kahvosh village in Dalahu county on 7 Khordad 1405.

Executed by the Islamic Republic's judiciary after being accused of espionage and intelligence activities on behalf of Israel. Accused of acting as one of Mossad's operational network leaders abroad and recruiting individuals inside Iran for anti-security operations. No independent details about the judicial process, evidence, access to legal counsel, or execution location were made public.

Went to the streets on 18 Dey (January 8, 2027) to protest and defend people. After 6 months of torture, harassment and various accusations, he was executed. He was described as very innocent, pure, and humble - someone who wouldn't hurt an ant and was loved by everyone. He grew up in hardship and suffered much in his life without anyone to support him.

Executed by the Islamic Republic at dawn on the morning of 3 Khordad, coinciding with the morning call to prayer. He was arrested, tried, and executed during internet blackouts. The entire process took less than 50 days and was rushed and unjust.

Kurdish merchant from Sardasht, shot directly in the head by Iranian government forces at close range without warning at Jasousan border crossing in Sardasht. He was critically injured and hospitalized in ICU at Khomeini Hospital in Mahabad before dying from his injuries. From Doletou village in Sardasht county.

Killed by her half-brother with an axe in a femicide case motivated by what family described as preservation of 'family honor.' Mother of five children and survivor of child marriage. Was allegedly given poisoned fruit juice and lost consciousness, then struck in the head with an axe by her half-brother, shattering her skull. Had endured years of infidelity and physical abuse during marriage before divorcing, worked as seamstress but lost livelihood due to war, struggled with rent and sought family support.
Kurdish political prisoner executed by hanging secretly at dawn in Naghideh central prison without family notification and deprived of final visitation

Shot with five bullets by a mentally disturbed individual while visiting her father's grave at Agha Seyyed Morteza cemetery in Lahijan at 5 PM with her brother's 7-year-old son. Died in hospital from gunshot wounds.
Kurdish political prisoner executed by hanging secretly at dawn in Naghideh central prison without family notification and deprived of final visitation

Executed by hanging in Qezelhesar Prison, Karaj. Convicted of murdering Elaheh Hosseinnajad with knife attacks, hiding the body, and destroying property including two mobile phones, a bag and identity documents.

Baloch citizen shot directly by government forces. He was riding his personal motorcycle when military forces targeted him with direct fire without any stop order or prior warning. After the initial bullet hit and the motorcycle overturned, military officers went over his head and shot him again from close range.

Executed by the Islamic Republic on charges of espionage and intelligence cooperation with Israel's Mossad. Civil engineering graduate and IT specialist who was arrested in early 2024 after returning from Turkey, held for months in solitary confinement before being transferred to general ward of Evin Prison. Human rights organizations stated that espionage charges were based on confessions extracted under torture and without credible evidence.
Sentenced to death by Branch 15 of Tehran's Revolutionary Court in connection with events on January 7 in Malard. Verdict reportedly based on forced confessions.

Executed by the Islamic Republic judiciary at dawn on Tuesday, May 12. Was arrested in Sistan and Baluchestan during the nationwide protests following the death of Mahsa Amini. Accused of being 'one of the key members of a terrorist group' but no evidence, documentation, or verified materials were provided to support the accusations. Death sentence was carried out after confirmation by the Supreme Court. No details were given regarding the date of the trial, the judicial process, the issuance of the sentence, or the circumstances surrounding its approval.

Turkish individual from Malekan in East Azerbaijan province, fatally shot by Basij forces stationed at a checkpoint in Bonab. Forces opened fire on his vehicle under the pretext that he had failed to obey an order to stop. He was struck by multiple bullets and died at the scene. Basij forces fired directly at the vehicle's passenger compartment without following legal procedures governing the use of firearms and without issuing any warning shots beforehand.

Shot without warning by Intelligence Ministry forces in direct shooting in Sarkohoran area of Iranshahr. Vehicle carrying the citizens caught fire and their bodies burned in the flames. After the vehicle stopped and fire occurred, the perpetrating forces shot again at the half-burned bodies to ensure their death. All four were married and fathers. Baloch citizen.

Shot in the head by government forces during protests in Narmak area of Tehran on January 8, 2027 (18 Dey). Severely injured and hospitalized in ICU at Imam Hussein Hospital for 123 days before dying. Father of a young daughter. When body was handed over to family, authorities claimed the shooter was 'terrorists' and suggested burial in martyrs' section of Behesht Zahra cemetery, but family opposed this.
Executed by the Islamic Republic. Was a Master's student in Aerospace Engineering, described as one of the brightest minds of Iran's young generation.

Shot without warning by Intelligence Ministry forces in direct shooting in Sarkohoran area of Iranshahr. Vehicle carrying the citizens caught fire and their bodies burned in the flames. After the vehicle stopped and fire occurred, the perpetrating forces shot again at the half-burned bodies to ensure their death. All four were married and fathers. Baloch citizen.

After the vehicle stopped and fire occurred, the perpetrating forces shot again at the half-burned bodies to ensure their death. All four were married and fathers. Baloch citizen.

Kurdish man killed in landmine explosion in the border highlands near Saqqez. Resident of Khorkhoreh village, was in the mountainous areas surrounding the village when a landmine exploded. The blast was so severe he was killed instantly.
55-year-old son of Shirmohammad from Mashhad, executed in Birjand prison on drug charges
Arrested after participating in protests in Dey 1404 in Mashhad, executed on 14 Ordibehesht 1405
Executed by hanging by the occupying regime of the Islamic Republic. Worker. Accused of having protest videos on his phone. Family was told the case was in the amnesty commission.
Executed by hanging by the occupying regime of the Islamic Republic. Worker. Accused of having protest videos on his phone. Family was told the case was in the amnesty commission.

Kurdish prisoner accused of 'espionage' who has been sentenced to death for the third time by the Islamic Revolutionary Court after his sentence was overturned twice by the Supreme Court. The court issued the death sentence again on Tuesday (February 3, 2026) despite the Supreme Court judges previously finding in two different rulings that espionage for Israel was not proven and the execution was overturned for multiple reasons.
Executed by hanging at dawn. Described as witnessing oppression in the silence of tyranny and dying to give credibility to the word 'freedom'

21-year-old karate athlete from Isfahan, arrested in Dey month and held in Dastgerd Prison in Isfahan. Executed by the Islamic Republic's judiciary. Charges against him included 'effective cooperation with the enemy, destruction of police force property, and inciting unrest'. Related to arrests from the 'Milk and Sun Revolution'.

Executed in Zahedan Central Prison. Baloch political prisoner who was arrested in Mehr month of 1403 (October 2024). Initially reported killed by government forces in the same year, but later it was revealed that Amer was wounded and arrested.

Executed by the Iranian regime after being sentenced to death on charges including 'Moharebeh' (enmity against God), 'creating insecurity,' 'inciting violent acts,' and 'participation in the destruction of public property.' He was arrested on January 8 during protests in the Piroozi Intersection area of Isfahan for his alleged role in damaging public property and arson. The execution was carried out in total media silence.
Injured on January 9, 2026 (19 Dey 1404) in Golshahr Karaj and died on April 24, 2026 (4 Farvardin 1405)

Former employee of Iran's Atomic Energy Organization, arrested by security forces in winter 1401, transferred from Tehran's Greater Prison to Evin Prison. Initially sentenced to 10 years in prison by Branch 26 of Tehran Revolutionary Court, but after another trial in Branch 23, sentenced to death on charges of espionage for Israel. The sentence was confirmed by the Supreme Court and executed.

Shot by IRGC forces without warning near Jakigur in Rask county. Killed instantly along with Mohammad Eslam Saeedi when IRGC forces opened direct fire on their civilian Land Cruiser vehicle. Both were from Darchub village in Sarkur area of Sarbaz county. Government media falsely labeled them as members of a 'terrorist team' to justify the extrajudicial killing.

Shot by IRGC forces without warning near Jakigur in Rask county. Killed instantly along with Mohammad Akram Akhtari when IRGC forces opened direct fire on their civilian Land Cruiser vehicle. Both were from Darchub village in Sarkur area of Sarbaz county. Government media falsely labeled them as members of a 'terrorist team' to justify the extrajudicial killing.

Executed by the Islamic Republic's judiciary. Charged with 'setting fire to the Qolhak Grand Mosque' and 'leading protests.' Described by regime media as one of the 'armed elements collaborating with the enemy.' Death sentence was carried out at dawn after being upheld by the Supreme Court. Had been identified by the IRGC Intelligence Organization. Allegedly confessed to participating in January 8 protests, damaging public property, and setting fire to a motorcycle, though circumstances of confession remain unclear. No prior information about his arrest, judicial proceedings, or detention was released before execution announcement.

Executed by hanging at dawn. Also known by the nickname Nima. Officially charged with espionage and moharebeh (enmity against God). Family claims he was not a member of MEK and his only crime was loyalty to the Pahlavi family and having different political views. Was prevented from saying goodbye to his lawyer and family due to prison silence.
Injured on January 9, 2026 in Arak, died on April 20, 2026
First Sergeant of Takavaran unit killed by direct gunfire from unknown armed individuals during a mission in Saravan city
Second Lieutenant of Takavaran unit killed by direct gunfire from unknown armed individuals during a mission in Saravan city
First Sergeant of Takavaran unit killed by direct gunfire from unknown armed individuals during a mission in Saravan city

Arrested from his mother's house at 2:30 AM on April 1st (12 Farvardin) after participating in protests on Kish Island. Died under severe torture in less than 24 hours in detention. His body was found at the morgue in Bandar Abbas on April 14th (25 Farvardin) with cause of death listed as 'unknown'. He was sentenced without trial and buried on April 18th (29 Farvardin) around Tehran under strict security measures.

Baloch teenager shot by IRGC forces while grazing livestock near the village of Bunser in the Kalgan border area of Golshan county. Critically wounded and left at the scene for several hours without medical assistance. Died from his injuries while being transported to a hospital after being found by his family.

Baloch teenager shot and killed by IRGC forces while grazing livestock near the village of Bunser in the Kalgan border area of Golshan county. Struck by two bullets to the chest and killed instantly. Body was transferred to a military outpost by government forces.

Killed by landmine explosion in border areas of Lar, Zahedan county. The landmine was planted by IRGC forces. His body was left in the area after the explosion and his family found it after two days of searching at border posts and morgues.

Arrested on January 8 in the Nezamabad area of Tehran. Family has been under pressure since his arrest not to speak publicly about his situation. A court branch presided over by Judge Abolghasem Salavati issued a death sentence without the presence of his chosen lawyer. Details of the charges and the judicial process have not been officially released. His name appeared on Amnesty International's list of urgent concern warning about the risk of heavy sentences and executions against protest detainees.

28-year-old Arab man with leg disability who was arrested from his tea shop in Zargan area of north Ahvaz during recent Iran-America-Israel conflict. He was the sole breadwinner for his family, running a small tea shop.

Brother of Sanaullah Pirasteh, shot by government forces without warning while in a moving vehicle in Sandhanzeh village, Dashtiari county. The vehicle carrying these individuals was hit by dozens of bullets, went off course and overturned, and one of the passengers was killed with a finishing shot after being wounded.

Shot by government forces without warning while in a moving vehicle in Sandhanzeh village, Dashtiari county. The vehicle carrying these individuals was hit by dozens of bullets, went off course and overturned, and one of the passengers was killed with a finishing shot after being wounded.

Shot and killed by Iranian government forces without warning while in a moving vehicle in the village of Sand Hamzeh. The vehicle was struck with dozens of bullets, veered off the road and overturned. One of the occupants who had been wounded was then killed by a final shot. Baloch ethnicity from the village of Samach. Brother of Abubakr Pirasteh.

Notes: Executed at dawn by the Islamic Republic judiciary. Had been arrested, imprisoned, and tortured during nationwide protests in January. Described as a teenage protester. Executed despite lack of sufficient evidence. Was the fifth person executed by the judiciary in three days amid a new wave of executions occurring simultaneously with ongoing war in Iran.
Baloch citizen from Rask county in Sistan and Balochistan province, killed by landmine explosion near Kastag border while working as a fuel smuggler

Kurdish man shot dead by Iranian Basij forces at a checkpoint at the entrance to Bijar city. He was holding a plastic toy pistol he had purchased for his nephew when security forces mistook it for a real weapon and opened fire, shooting dozens of rounds. He was struck by multiple bullets in the head, neck, chest and thigh. He had recently been granted temporary leave from prison.

23-year-old Arab youth from Om-Tamir area of Ahvaz, shot and killed by direct gunfire from government forces (Basij and plainclothes agents) who opened fire on his vehicle without prior warning in Kianpars area of Ahvaz, allegedly for not stopping at a checkpoint
Injured on January 8, 2026 in Mashhad, died on March 30, 2026
Shot by forces stationed at a Basij checkpoint

Kurdish civilian shot dead by Iranian security forces during a nighttime vehicle pursuit in the Chaharbagh neighborhood. Security forces chased his Peugeot vehicle without warning, fired approximately 12 bullets at the car, then shot him twice in the back of the head after the vehicle stopped. Forces claimed they mistook him for an 'Israeli' or 'Zionist' during the Iran-Israel-US war. He had returned from Karaj to Sanandaj for his engagement preparations.

Young Kurdish man from the village of Kamiz near Sanandaj, shot and killed by Iranian government forces while traveling with his parents in a private vehicle on the road from Qolian village to Sanandaj. Plainclothes forces opened fire on the vehicle without prior warning, striking him with two bullets and critically injuring him. He died from the severity of his injuries before reaching a medical facility.
Shot directly on January 8, 2026 in Narmak, was hospitalized until March 18, 2026 when he died from his injuries. Had a 10-year-old daughter.

Shot by Islamic Republic security forces during protests in Fardis on January 8, 2026. War bullet hit his pelvis and leg area. After being taken to Sardar Soleimani Hospital, doctors had to cut the main artery of his leg to prevent bleeding due to lack of specialist physician. He was later transferred to Omid Hospital and then to Taleghani Hospital where his right leg was amputated above the thigh after 24 hours. A bullet that remained in his right pelvis for 36 hours was surgically removed. He suffered for 72 days in Taleghani Hospital before being discharged on March 19, 2026, against his wife's wishes at doctors' insistence. He died in his wife's arms just three hours after returning home. Father of two children.

Father of two children, shot with military bullet by regime suppression forces on January 8, 2026 in Fardis, Karaj. Transferred to Sardar Soleimani Hospital in Malard where doctors could not repair the artery in his leg due to lack of transplant surgeon, so they severed the main artery to prevent bleeding. Family was not allowed to transfer him to Tehran initially. Next day transferred to Omid Hospital Tehran, then to Taleghani Hospital in Velenjak when his leg turned black. After 24 hours, his right leg was amputated above the thigh. Next day they operated again to remove the bullet that had remained in his right pelvis for 36 hours. Fought for 72 days in Taleghani Hospital with great pain and suffering. Discharged on March 19, 2026 against his wife's wishes at doctors' insistence. Was home for only 3 hours before dying in his wife's arms.

Executed by public hanging on charges of 'Moharebeh' (enmity against God) and alleged killing of two police officers during protests on January 8, 2026. Shot by government forces in Zahedan. Known by the nickname 'Jamook'. Killed during operations where 4 others were arrested in connection with him around the city, and 13 other citizens were arrested in Iranshahr, Zahedan and Chabahar on charges of alleged 'cooperation with Israel'.

Executed by public hanging on charges of 'Moharebeh' (enmity against God) and alleged killing of two police officers during protests on January 8, 2026. Notes: 19-year-old resident of Qom city, holder of bronze medal from Russia's international 'SCITF' cup in 2024 and runner-up in international Abdullah Movahed competitions. Arrested on January 15, 2026 by security forces following participation in protests in Dey month 1404. Criminal court of Qom sentenced him to death on February 3, 2026 on charges of 'murder' of a special police unit officer and 'moharebeh' (enmity against God). The sentence was carried out publicly in Qom city on March 19, 2026.

43-year-old from Kashmar, resident of Mashhad, civil activist and protester with history of arrests and violent encounters. Previously arrested during 2009 protests and severely injured and tortured. Was present during Dey 1404 protests where a friend was killed near him, after which he went into hiding. Disappeared on 24 Esfand 1404 after leaving his home in Mashhad. Hours later, family was called to police station and shown image of his lifeless body. Sources describe this as a targeted and premeditated action, saying he was under pressure and surveillance beforehand. Buried on 26 Esfand 1404 in security atmosphere in his birthplace Kashmar.

Shot in the neck by government forces on Keshavarz Boulevard on January 9, 2026 (19 Dey) while sheltering protesters and closing her door. The shooting caused spinal cord severance and she died approximately two months later from complications of the injury. Buried in Baghe Rezvan cemetery, Isfahan, section 52.

Kurdish citizen from Saqez killed by direct gunfire from IRGC forces around Baneh city. IRGC forces surrounded a private vehicle and immediately opened fire, killing the driver Arman Khalegpanah. His body has not been returned to his family.
Was injured on January 3, 2026 in Khorramabad and died on March 10, 2026

23-year-old protester from Dey month protests. After being identified, he was shot by regime mercenaries on 16 Esfand in front of his house door in Pardis neighborhood of Jam city, Bushehr province, and died on 17 Esfand.
Shot by forces stationed at a Basij checkpoint in Shahinshahr, Isfahan

Shot by suppressive forces on the street with gunfire. He was standing on the street when they confronted him and opened fire. His body was handed over to his family three days later. He lived only with his mother, was the breadwinner of the house and worked while studying.

Graduate of Amirkabir University of Technology with a Master's degree in Chemical Engineering (admitted 1401/2022), recently sent for military service. Killed during an attack on the military base where he was serving in Baneh. Had completed his training at Army Base 01 and was then transferred to Baneh to continue his service. Family emphasizes he had no organizational affiliation or activity within government institutions.
27-year-old Kurdish student from Sanandaj, pursued by plainclothes forces in a masked Peugeot 405 in Qazvin on the evening of 10 Esfand 1404 and killed by direct gunfire

Engineer shot directly in the head with pellet gun by government security forces during street celebrations following news of Ali Khamenei's death. She was with her brother getting medicine for their cancer-stricken mother in Baharestan area of Isfahan when protests erupted. Security forces surrounded their car, broke the rear window, and an officer placed his weapon directly on her temple and fired.

Shot by Islamic Republic agents while in car with father and brother after honking horn to spread news of Ali Khamenei's death. Car was subjected to heavy gunfire and shooting continued even after the vehicle stopped. Died from gunshot wounds. Medical examiner report stated cause of death as 'hot bullet impact to torso'. Buried on 13 Esfand at Behesht Ali cemetery in Eshtehard.

Shot by Islamic Republic agents while in car with father and brother after honking horn to spread news of Ali Khamenei's death. Car was subjected to heavy gunfire and shooting continued even after the vehicle stopped. Died from gunshot wounds. Medical examiner report stated cause of death as 'hot bullet impact to torso'. Buried on 13 Esfand at Behesht Ali cemetery in Eshtehard.
Was a soldier returning home on 19 Dey in Andisheh when an attack occurred. A woman had been shot and he went to help her, then was shot in the head. Was taken to hospital and died this afternoon.
Shot directly in the chest with a war bullet by Islamic Republic mercenaries while celebrating in the streets of Khorramabad with friends after Khamenei's death. Also known as Kowsar.

Shot dead by Iranian government forces who opened fire on a vehicle near Keyo Square in Khorramabad after she expressed joy over Khamenei's death. She was inside the vehicle with several friends when killed. Her body was returned to her family three days later and authorities pressured the family to register her as a 'war martyr' but the family refused.
Was wounded on January 9, 2027 in Mashhad, died from his injuries on February 27, 2027. Described as a child of Iran and defender of the homeland.

20-year-old athlete from Najafabad, Isfahan, who was severely injured on January 8th by a war bullet fired by Islamic Republic agents to the head. He died after 50 days in intensive care on Thursday evening, February 26th. He was a physical education student at Najafabad University and a protester against the Islamic Republic. His father sold his only asset, a car, to pay money to remove Mehdi's name from the registered injured list to prevent his arrest.
Shot directly in the head by Islamic regime suppressors on 19 Dey month in Yazdanshahr, Isfahan. Was in hospital for about 4 weeks and died in hospital on 13 Esfand.
Shot in the eye on January 8th (18 Dey), remained in coma for 50 days before dying on February 23rd (4 Esfand). Family requested that their child not remain anonymous.
Arrested on 18 Dey (January 8) in Eslamshahr and executed the day before the tweet. 17-18 years old at time of death.
Executed in Semnan Central Prison for murder charges. Father of two children, had been detained for about four years following a conflict.
Injured on January 8, 2026 in Haft-e-Howz area of Tehran, died on February 21, 2026
Shot on January 8, 2026 in Mashhad. Father brought him to Tehran to avoid getting caught, but he was arrested there. Sentenced to death by executioner judges on February 20, 2026 and executed.

19-year-old Baloch man from Mehrestan County in Sistan and Baluchestan Province, died under torture in Intelligence Ministry custody. Father of a six-month-old child, resident of Kont village in Sib and Soran County. Killed as a result of severe torture inflicted by Intelligence Ministry agents. Visible signs of severe physical abuse were found on his body, including fractures and burn marks. Died as a result of intense beatings during detention, with reported injuries including fractures to his mouth, teeth, and legs. Had been arrested on February 17, 2026, in front of a repair shop in Mehrestan.

Baloch citizen killed under torture by intelligence officers in Mehrestan intelligence detention center. Arrested on Tuesday 28 Bahman in front of a repair shop in Mehrestan city and transferred to intelligence detention center. Father of a six-month-old child from Kant village in Sib va Suran county. Body showed signs of fractures and burns, with reported fractures to mouth, teeth and legs from severe beatings during detention. Family was called and threatened before body was handed over.

35-year-old protester from Mashhad who died under torture after more than one month of detention. He was arrested on 22 Dey 1404 (2026-01-12) during nationwide protests in Mashhad. After more than a month in prison, he died as a result of torture. Intelligence agents arrested him by raiding his home. After more than a month, authorities contacted his family saying he was in a coma at Velayat Hospital. When the family visited the hospital, they found his physical condition was very critical and both his hands were broken. His death was announced to his family on the first of Esfand. His body was buried on Saturday, 2 Esfand at Behesht-e Reza cemetery in Mashhad.
Injured on January 9, 2026 in Qeshm and died on February 19

Killed by her son-in-law with a knife and brick, struck multiple times in the head and body. Originally from Dehdasht in Kohgiluyeh and Boyer-Ahmad Province, was visiting her daughter in Ardakan when attacked after leaving a mosque following a brief argument. The assault was captured by surveillance cameras.

Shot directly by government forces during a memorial ceremony for protest victims (Chehlom ceremony). While fleeing the scene on a motorcycle with friends after government forces attacked the gathering, he was targeted by direct gunfire from government forces. Three bullets hit his heart, head, and side. Government authorities falsely claimed his death was due to an accident, but the family was able to see the body and confirmed bullet wounds. The body was buried under strict security measures without public funeral ceremonies.

Baloch citizen kidnapped on February 11, 2026 from Mozari Street in Zahedan by armed individuals. After four days of severe torture, he was abandoned in front of a hospital on February 15, 2026. Died on February 18 due to severity of injuries, kidney failure, and torture-related damage. Son of Ahmad, resident of Nokchah village in Zahedan county. Kidnappers injected drugs that caused his kidney failure, and his body showed signs of burns and severe torture indicating extensive violence.
Injured on January 9, 2026 in Qeshm and died on February 18
Executed in Malayer Central Prison. Lor citizen from Ki Kian village in Malayer district, father of one child. Had been detained for about five years for murdering his wife.

Former army member who had resigned. During the national revolution on 18 Dey, suffered brain death from a sound bomb explosion near his face but was kept alive on life support machines until he died a few days before this tweet. Body was buried in Mashhad.

21-year-old veterinary student at Chamran University of Ahvaz who died suspiciously just days after being released from detention. He was arrested on 2026-01-08 during protests and released on 2026-02-03 after more than a month in prison. Sources close to him reported he was not in good mental and physical condition after release, and friends said he was tortured during detention. He suffered a heart attack in his sleep and died. There have been multiple reports of torture and injection of suspicious drugs to detainees of the Lion and Sun Revolution of Iran, with an increasing number of people dying shortly after release.

Shot in the head by regime forces' bullets on Thursday, 18 Dey in Saman city, Chaharmahal and Bakhtiari province. Fell into a coma and after enduring days of suffering, finally died on 25 Bahman 1404.
Executed in Kashmar Prison for murder charges. Was arrested 16 months earlier when he was 18 years and 2 months old.
Was injured on 2026-01-08 in Tehran and died on 2026-02-14 after more than a month of fighting severe injuries caused by gunshot wounds
Was injured on January 8, 2026 in Tehran and died on February 13, 2027

Baloch civil activist who went missing for three days in Sib va Suran county while traveling with his family. His body was discovered by local people with gunshot wounds. He was a civil activist from Mehrestan and Sib va Suran counties who pursued civic demands to help resolve citizens' problems.

Kurdish man from village of Sartang in Eyvan-e Gharb, Ilam Province. Killed under torture following arrest by Iranian intelligence agents in Bandar Abbas on February 6, 2026. After several days in custody at a detention facility, he was killed under severe torture. Authorities claimed he 'committed suicide.' Had previously been a member of Komala Party of Kurdistan for two years in Kurdistan Region of Iraq, left the party prior to 'Women, Life, Freedom' movement and returned to Iran. Became active during the movement and was previously arrested and interrogated. Was employed as a laborer at Shahid Rajaee Port. Family was threatened against speaking publicly about the killing.

Hairdresser who participated in protests on January 9, 2026 in Bandar Anzali but never returned home. His body was returned to his family 34 days later showing severe signs of torture. The official date of death was given as December 29, 2025, though he left home to join protests on January 9, 2026. His body showed extensive injuries and severe beatings throughout, with part of his skull crushed. There was bruising on his face and around his eyes, abnormal condition and bruising of his hands indicating they were broken, and tears visible in his stomach area. His mother was threatened by security and judicial officials to either accept the body or have him buried as an unidentified person.

Environmental activist and mountaineering athlete who was shot during Dey 1404 protests in Isfahan. Initially wounded and transferred to Kashan, he later died from the severity of his injuries. He had traveled to Isfahan on 18 Dey to visit close relatives when he was injured.

13-year-old Kurdish child killed by landmine explosion left over from Iran-Iraq war in Cham-Hendi area of Dehloran county in Ilam province. Died on site due to severity of injuries sustained in the explosion that occurred on Tuesday evening.

Shot in the abdomen with a war bullet on 18th of Dey month in Kerman, severely wounded. After one month of brave struggle and enduring six heavy surgeries, including removal of one kidney and part of intestines, died on 19 Bahman due to stomach bleeding and severe blood infection.
Arrested on February 2, 2026 (13 Bahman 1404) and his body was delivered to his family on February 9, 2026 (20 Bahman)
Executed in Spidar Prison Ahvaz. Convicted of premeditated murder following a street dispute in Ahvaz city, arrested about 3 years ago. From Bandar Abbas.

17-year-old breadwinner for his family. Violently arrested by intelligence forces on February 8th from his workplace (tile production line). No news of him since then and no institution has been responsive. His father has suffered seizures due to fear and psychological pressure.

Shot with pellets in throat and back on January 9, 2026 in Resalat area of Tehran. Several hospitals including Ansari Hospital refused to admit him, but Resalat Hospital finally admitted him. He remained hospitalized and underwent several surgeries before dying on February 8. He had recently gotten engaged and told those who brought him to the hospital 'I don't want to die. I just got married.'
Executed in Spidar Prison Ahvaz. Convicted of premeditated murder following a street dispute in Ahvaz city, arrested about 3 years ago in joint case with Amrollah Sajjadi. From Bandar Abbas.
Executed in Arak Central Prison. Convicted of drug-related crimes, arrested about 2 years ago. From Saveh.

20-year-old soldier from Neyshabur who was stationed in Sistan. Had finished his training and returned to Neyshabur. Had posted Instagram stories about the national revolution of the people. After returning to his unit, a few days later they contacted his family saying their son had caught cold and died. His body was buried under security measures.
Executed in Gorgan Central Prison. Convicted of murder following a dispute and conflict, arrested about 3 years ago. From Gonbad-e Kavus.

Hanged in his shop by government forces on the night of 18 Bahman, one month after the suppression of the Dey month uprising. His death was falsely labeled as 'suicide'. Security cameras around his shop were collected and taken away to prevent the truth from being revealed.
Executed in Gorgan Central Prison. Convicted of murder during a street dispute, arrested about 3 years ago.

16-year-old resident of Tous Boulevard in Mashhad, wounded on Friday January 9, 2026 during protests by war bullets to his abdomen and side area. He underwent more than 15 surgical operations until Tuesday February 3, 2026, but died due to widespread infection.

15-year-old arrested by Islamic Republic forces at his school on 12 Bahman. Family was contacted a few days later to collect their child's body. Family has been threatened that if they speak about this matter, their other son will also be in danger.

Baloch citizen shot and killed by unknown armed individuals while traveling from Chabahar to Zahedan near Kahnook village in Iranegan district of Khash county at approximately 3:30 AM. Son of Abdel, married, resident of Zahedan.
Executed in Gorgan Central Prison. Convicted of murder, arrested about 6 years ago. From Gorgan, father of two children.

51-year-old from Fasa in Fars province. Shot with war bullets by Islamic Republic agents in Shiraz on January 9, 2026, was wounded and later died in hospital. According to family members, suppressive agents threatened the family and forced them to sign pledges, then falsely introduced Abdolali Radmanesh as a 'government martyr'. He was the son of Ghobad Radmanesh, who died in the Iran-Iraq war.

Gilaki citizen from Ramsar and father of an 8-year-old son. Shot with a Kalashnikov war bullet to the abdomen by Islamic Republic forces on January 8, 2026 (18 Dey 1404) in front of the IRGC building in Ramsar during nationwide protests. Was severely injured and transferred to Ramsar hospital where he underwent several surgeries over several weeks. Despite medical efforts, his physical condition deteriorated and he died in the hospital.

26-year-old Kurdish (Kurmanji) man living in Rasht who was severely wounded by direct shotgun pellets fired by Islamic Republic suppression forces during popular protests on January 9, 2026 (19 Dey 1404). After enduring his injuries for 25 days, he died at Poursina Hospital in Rasht. He had recently become a father and his child was only two months old. His body was buried under strict security measures at Bagh-e Rezvan cemetery in Rasht.
18 years old, arrested on January 9th, died on February 1st. Final cause of death announced as gunshot wound. From Ghazeghan village in Mashhad.
Shot and wounded on the night of January 6th at Chanarshahijan roundabout. Friends took him home for treatment but he died at home on February 1st due to infection from his wounds.

Singer, father of one daughter, Gilaki citizen from Astaneh-ye Ashrafiyeh. Shot by regime suppression forces on Thursday January 8, 2026, severely wounded and died about two weeks later from the severity of his injuries
Shot directly by Islamic Republic agents in Fardis. He was initially wounded, then abducted, and after 23 days his lifeless body was returned to his family.

Father of two children from Surak city in Mazandaran province. Was injured on Thursday January 8, 2026 during popular protests in Sari's Martyrs Square when government forces shot him directly with a live round that hit his leg. Died two weeks later on February 21, 2026 due to delayed hospital transfer and severe infection. Had avoided seeking timely medical treatment due to fear of arrest.

Shot with pellets by suppressive forces during protests on 18 Dey 1404 (January 8, 2026) in Isfahan while defending his wife Shirin. After being knocked down, several armed forces aimed their weapons at him and fired pellets consecutively, leaving over 200 pellets in his body and causing him to lose feeling in his legs immediately. He was left for dead by the attacking forces but survived initially. Despite being transferred to hospital and undergoing surgery with initial relative improvement, he eventually died on 11 Bahman 1404 due to the severity of injuries and complications from the numerous pellets. He had been transferred home from hospital before completing full treatment due to fear of being finished off or arrested. Radiological images showed many pellets had hit sensitive areas of his body. Married man.

Father of family, arrested on 18 Dey during Iranian national revolution in Sadeghiyeh, killed with a coup de grâce shot after six days of torture in detention center of the Islamic Republic, family under severe pressure and forced silence

Bakhtiari ethnicity, arrested in Kerman city and killed in custody. Body returned to family on 10 Bahman 1404 and buried in Fooladshahr, Isfahan where his family originally lived. Disappeared on 18 or 19 Dey month after not returning home, later confirmed arrested. Family received body after weeks of no information. Initial family examination showed no bullet wounds, cuts, or visible external injuries on the body, reducing likelihood of death from direct physical assault and increasing doubts about actual cause of death. Family believes he died during detention and suspects death may have resulted from forced injection or forced drug consumption.

Baloch citizen who was severely beaten by unknown individuals. Found in critical condition on a Tehran street on Friday, January 29, 2026, and died after being taken to hospital. Forensic examination showed death was caused by severe blows to the head with a hard object. Evidence showed he had been severely beaten before death. Son of Ali Mohammad, from Mitgan village in Sarbaz county. Worked as a welder. Left his residence Thursday evening January 28 after receiving a phone call from someone claiming to need him 'for work'. Multiple signs of beating were observed on various parts of his body including head, abdomen, legs and genital areas during burial. Body was buried in his hometown of Sarbaz on Monday February 2, 2026.
Young man from a wealthy family whose parents were both doctors and whose father was an experienced and well-known plastic surgeon. He gave his life for Iran.

Technology and computer specialist who prevented surveillance cameras from identifying protesters. Found dead in his private residence on 9 Bahman. Family and close associates believe he was murdered by government agencies, not suicide as officially claimed. Before his body was found, a suspicious person entered his residential building and an unknown vehicle was parked around the area for some time. He was talented in computer field, unmarried, and preparing for educational migration to Germany.
Executed in Taybad Prison. Afghan national from Herat city, convicted of drug-related charges, arrested about 2 years ago.
Was injured on January 8, 2026 (18 Dey 1404) in Mashhad and died on January 29, 2026 (9 Bahman 1404)

18-year-old Baluch citizen, son of Abdolkarim, resident of Gazhak village in Delgan county. Shot and killed by unknown armed individuals around 2 AM while traveling by personal vehicle in Chahshoor village area. Died on the spot due to severity of injuries from multiple gunshot wounds. Attackers fled immediately after the shooting.
Executed in Taybad Prison. Convicted of drug-related charges, arrested about 3 years ago. From Taybad.

16-year-old child from Ardabil and wushu athlete, died after 21 days in a coma due to injuries from being shot with military bullets by government suppression forces. He was wounded while trying to save a girl who was being directly targeted during protests on Thursday 18 Dey in Ardabil. His body was buried at 7 AM on 10 Bahman.

19-year-old who disappeared on Friday 19 Dey, was returned to his family after 20 days on 9 Bahman with signs of torture on his body from Kahrizak. His body was buried in Behesht Zahra cemetery with his family and amid applause.
Executed in Shiraz Central Prison (Adel-Abad). Gilak prisoner from Rasht, convicted of drug-related crimes, arrested about 1 year and 5 months ago. Father of four children.

Captain and border guard officer originally from Kalaleh in Golestan Province, killed in landmine explosion in Sirkan area of Saravan County, Sistan and Baluchestan Province. The landmines were reportedly planted by the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) to block movement of Baloch fuel carriers (Sukhtbars). Died from severity of injuries after being taken to hospital.
Border guard killed by mine explosion in Sirkan area of Saravan. Originally from Kalaleh city in Golestan province. Died at hospital due to severity of injuries sustained from the mine blast.
Executed in Yazd Central Prison. Convicted of drug-related charges, arrested 2 years ago. From Yazd, father of one child.
Shot with war bullet in the abdomen on Taleghani Street in Tehran, underwent bowel and stomach evacuation surgery at hospital, fell into coma, and died after 10 days
Executed in Shiraz Central Prison (Adel-Abad). Afghan national from Baghlan province, convicted of premeditated murder, arrested about 2 years ago.
Executed in Shiraz Central Prison (Adel-Abad). Gilak prisoner from Bashman village in Anzali district, convicted of drug-related crimes, arrested 4 years ago.
Executed in Shiraz Central Prison (Adel-Abad). Gilak prisoner from Rasht, convicted of drug-related crimes, arrested 3 years ago.
Executed in Shiraz Central Prison (Adel-Abad). Afghan national from Baghlan province, convicted of premeditated murder, arrested about 2 years ago.

Eight-year-old child shot without justification by police forces in Taheruiyeh neighborhood while a police vehicle was pursuing a car suspected of carrying smuggled goods. The child was an innocent bystander who was hit by gunfire. After being taken to the hospital, police surrounded the hospital and prevented news and details of the incident from being published. The incident led to widespread anger among citizens.

17-year-old Lor child from Chaghakhor village in Chaharmahal and Bakhtiari province, resident of Shahin Shahr Isfahan. Shot in the leg with a war bullet during popular protests by direct fire from government forces on Thursday 18 Dey 1404 (January 8, 2026). Was wounded and arrested, then died in custody. His body was returned to the family after 20 days of repeated follow-ups by the family. Body was buried on Friday 10 Bahman 1404 in his birthplace, Chaghakhor district.

Executed on charges of espionage for Israel. The judiciary claimed he conducted intelligence and operational activities for the Zionist regime including purchasing equipment needed by officers, moving vehicles in Isfahan and Lorestan provinces to prepare for Israeli sabotage of Defense Ministry missile facilities in an operation called 'Great Operation' that was discovered and neutralized by Intelligence Ministry agents in 1401 (2022-2023).
Shot with a war bullet on Behjouyi Street in Ardabil on 19 Dey, severely wounded and died after enduring injuries for several days

Shot at close range with pellet ammunition on January 10, 2026 (19 Dey). Remained in a coma from the time of shooting until death on January 26, 2026 (6 Bahman) due to severity of injuries. Born in village of Eskaneh near Arak.

Teacher and official education employee with master's degree in psychology. Shot directly with military bullet to the head on Thursday January 8, 2026 (18 Dey) in Marlik area. Bullet hit his brain causing severe injury. Was kept alive on life support for one full week in coma with very low consciousness at Shohada-ye Salamat Malard Hospital. IRGC forces prevented organ donation despite his organ donor card, stating those shot during protests cannot donate organs. Family was demanded approximately 2 billion tomans as 'bullet fee' after autopsy to determine weapon type. Died after one week without being allowed to donate organs despite being healthy and suitable for donation. Body was eventually returned to family and buried in Behesht-e Fatemeh cemetery in Qods city.

Shot in the throat in Fardis on Thursday 18 Dey month. The bullet damaged his trachea, lungs, and spinal cord. After surgery and regaining consciousness in the hospital, he was unable to speak or move his legs. His eyes could not see due to the shotgun pellet injury. He ultimately died due to the severity of his injuries after nearly two weeks on 5 Bahman. He was a Lor from Malmir village, living in Fardis, Karaj. He was a mechanic who worked on heavy vehicles and was the father of a 2-year-old child. His hardworking hands were still black when he was on the hospital bed. His family was under pressure to have a quiet burial at 7 AM without putting up his photo anywhere.
Employee of Landa laboratory from Gorgan. Shot with war bullets by the regime on 18 Dey 1404 (January 8, 2026), wounded and died after one week of suffering severe injuries in hospital.
Shot with war bullets on 18 Dey in Tehran, taken to hospital and underwent surgery, but died on 6 Bahman. Originally from Sabzevar city.
Became martyred on Mokhtari Street in Amiriyeh district of Tehran on Saturday night of the 20th. Witnessed describes seeing his eyes bulging out of their sockets in his final moments

Location of Death: Siahan border area, Sistan and Baluchestan
Notes: 16-year-old teenager shot by border guards with bullet hitting head area. Killed alongside 20-year-old Yasin Siahani in the Siahan border area during heightened alert status following nationwide protest suppression. Border guards were shooting without warning or identification at anyone seen near zero point of border. Both victims were local residents using the mountainous Siahan route between Bacheh-rahi and Esfandak for daily commute.

Lor Bakhtiari citizen from Nanadgan village in Fereydan, Isfahan province. Shot and killed by Basij forces while alone in his barbershop on Felatori Street, Iman Boulevard in Isfahan. He was targeted with two pistol shots from close range. He had previously been identified by local Basij forces on 18 Dey month. Was unmarried and buried in his birthplace in Nanadgan village, Fereydan.

Location of Death: Siahan border area, Sistan and Baluchestan
Notes: Shot by border guards with bullet hitting head area. Killed alongside 16-year-old Nader Siahani in the Siahan border area during heightened alert status following nationwide protest suppression. Border guards were shooting without warning or identification at anyone seen near zero point of border. Both victims were local residents using the mountainous Siahan route between Bacheh-rahi and Esfandak for daily commute.

Shot by Islamic Republic forces on January 8, 2026, sustaining severe injuries to shoulder, hand, and foot. Was hospitalized from that date until his death on January 24, 2026, when he was killed by direct gunfire from Islamic Republic forces. His family was pressured to declare the cause of death as illness and forced to bury him at night with limited attendance (less than 15 people) after posting a 4 billion toman bond.
Shot directly by government suppressive forces during protests. Originally from Dezful, resident of Isfahan.
Was injured on 19 Dey 1404 in Chamgardan, Isfahan and died on 1 Bahman
Brave nurse and mother of two who became a human shield to save young protesters from bullets
Wounded on 19 Dey 1404 in Mashhad and died on 4 Bahman
17-year-old who was injured on 18 Dey 1404 in Bushehr and died on 3 Bahman. Born in 1372 Persian calendar.
Shot with pellets on the 18th, his kidneys and lungs were punctured, died on 4 Bahman. Father of three children. From Fasa.

14-year-old Kurdish child who was critically wounded during protests when government forces shot her directly with a war bullet that hit her pelvis area. After two weeks in a coma, she died due to severe injuries and inadequate medical care. She was an honor student at Setaregan School in Kermanshah. Her body was buried on Sunday 5 Bahman under pressure and threats from security agencies.

Citizen from Sari, barista at Melo Cafe on Mahdieh Street, shot by government suppressive forces at his workplace during popular protests. Initially injured and hospitalized, died on Wednesday 24 Dey due to severity of injuries
Gilak citizen from Amlash killed during nationwide protests in Dey month 1404 by direct gunfire from Islamic Republic of Iran regime agents. The shooting occurred on either 18 or 19 Dey 1404.
Was injured on 18 Dey in Tehranpars. After two weeks in a coma at Al-Ghadir Hospital, he died.

19-year-old Baháʼí resident of Kerman arrested on Saturday evening 4 Bahman 1404 when security forces brutally raided her home without showing a warrant. House was searched before arrest. Arrest was part of widespread detention of Baháʼí citizens during protests. No information available about her since arrest.

33-year-old reportedly executed in Mashhad on 22 January, becoming one of the first protesters to be executed. Iran's judiciary denied his execution having taken place, stating that such a person had not even been detained

Left home at 3 AM after celebrating his birthday (2nd Bahman). His phone was in possession of security agencies. The next morning his body was found hanging in the house courtyard with his mouth covered by cloth and hands and feet tied. Forensic results have not been announced. Milad had said he was being followed.

Arrested on January 8, 2026 by Islamic Republic agents and transferred to Evin prison. He contacted his family from prison saying he was fine and that they had asked for money for his release. After torture and suffocation, he was killed by direct shooting from Islamic Republic agents (fatal shot above the ear). His body was returned to the family after two weeks. He was buried in Behesht Zahra on his birthday.

Shot in the head with a war bullet in front of Gohardasht police station. Music teacher and tar/setar instructor, manager of 'Pazhvak Honar' music school. His body was missing for 7 days before being found mistakenly buried in place of another person at Behesht-e Sakineh. Body found with torn clothes and blood, face unrecognizable due to severe injuries, identified only through fingerprints. Father was pressured to not hold a ceremony and falsely claim Yaser was a Basij member, though he was an independent artist and musician.
Injured on 18 Dey 1404 in Mashhad, died on 21 Dey

39-year-old citizen of Mashhad and father of an 11-year-old daughter, wounded by Khamenei's agents' bullets in front of Haft-e Tir police station in Mashhad on 18 Dey 1404, died in hospital 3 days later. His Instagram page showed he was also a supporter of the Pahlavi dynasty.

48-year-old resident of Babol and father, shot directly by suppressive forces during nationwide protests on 18 Dey (first day of Crown Prince Reza Pahlavi's call) in the city. A shotgun pellet hit his eye from close range, causing severe brain hemorrhage, and he died after three days in a coma.

Nurse at Imam Reza Hospital in Kermanshah, internal medicine ward. Shot by a sniper positioned in a tall building above Dr. Kaboli pharmacy near Housing Bank at the city four-way intersection (Doveqola) in Shaabad. He was targeted from behind. Taken home initially but after a few hours had to be transferred to hospital. Died after three days of resistance.

Killed in drone and missile attack on Kurdistan Freedom Party (PAK) logistics and support forces positions near Kawrasor village in Erbil province. One drone landed and exploded near residential houses. Attack attributed to Islamic Republic of Iran or its proxy forces in Iraq.

Baluch citizen who was severely wounded on 24 Dey 1404 during protests in Mashhad by shotgun pellets fired by military forces to the head, neck, chest and abdomen. He hid for 8 days out of fear of arrest and finally died on 1 Bahman.

Baloch citizen killed by security and military forces. Former wushu champion, coach, referee and veteran in Sistan and Baluchistan province. Former political prisoner who was arrested in 2010 in Zahedan prison, sentenced to 20 years exile in Gachsaran prison for charges including 'moharebeh and corruption on earth', sent on leave in 2022 after serving over 12 years of prison and exile, and had been residing in Khajeh Mask village in Zahedan county after refusing to return to prison. Government media claimed he was killed in clashes on the outskirts of Zahedan and labeled him as a member of Ansar al-Furqan group, but the group officially denied any connection with him.

Husband of Shehla Kakaei, also known as Khabat Kakaei. Was wounded during the same shooting incident that killed his wife on January 8. After being discharged from hospital and struggling with the psychological impact of his wife's killing, he died by suicide. The couple were cousins. He was buried on January 21 at the Haji Ebrahim Cemetery in Javanrud under heavy security presence.
Said to have died in Karaj and buried in Hamadan, but details are unconfirmed as the channel is seeking more information

Flower seller and father of a 7-year-old son who was severely injured on January 9, 2026 in Eslamshahr by a Doshka heavy machine gun bullet to the abdomen. He was hospitalized for 10 days and underwent three surgical operations, but the severity of his injuries led to his death on January 20, 2026.

From Salmas, wounded and arrested during protests in Tehran. His body was returned to his family 12 days later, on Tuesday, January 20, without any explanation. Despite persistent follow-up by the family after his arrest, they received no information about his fate until they were summoned by authorities and handed his body.

Wounded by shotgun pellet on January 8 in Golshahr, Karaj. After his arrest, he was taken to Qezel Hesar prison, and on January 20, his family was informed that they could retrieve his body from Behesht-e Sakineh cemetery.
Shot by Islamic Republic suppression forces during direct fire on protesters in Lordegan

Shot with war bullet by suppressors in his side at Fatemiyeh Mashhad (Imamiyeh police station). He was a bodybuilding trainer and family counselor with a master's degree from Ferdowsi University, a top university student.

19-year-old from Malek Shahr who participated in protests on 18-19 Dey month. After participating in protests, he received threatening calls from security agencies. On 28 Dey month, while working as a Snapp driver, he was kidnapped by unknown individuals in Shahin Shahr area. Local sources say he died after being injected with a toxic substance following his abduction. His body was found hours later on the side of a street in Shahin Shahr. His family has been threatened by security forces to remain silent about his death.

Shot by security forces in Shahriar on Thursday January 8, 2026, hit by bullet in the back/waist area. After being transferred to hospital by security forces, he died on January 18, 2026. His physical condition after being shot was severely critical and widespread infection in the body, along with punctured lung, were declared as the main causes of death. Funeral held under strict security measures with plainclothes forces present among people, preventing public ceremony. Buried in Behesht-e Rezvan cemetery in Shahriar.

Kurdish citizen from Shabad, Eslamabad Gharb (Shabad), Kermanshah province. Truck driver who was shot by Islamic Republic of Iran government forces during protests in the city. He was wounded on Thursday, 18 Dey month and died in hospital after 5 days due to the severity of injuries from bullets fired by suppressors. Government institutions pressured his family to blame Kurdish parties for his death, which they refused. Suppressive government institutions demanded 700 million tomans for the return of his body.
Shot with 3 live bullets by Islamic regime forces - one in her side, one in her arm, one in her leg
Intervened on January 9th to prevent a girl from being arrested, was himself arrested. His body was delivered on January 18th with a bullet wound to his forehead (execution-style shot)
Disappeared on 20 Dey 1404 (2026-01-10) after participating in Dey month protests in Mashhad. His body was found 8 days later on 28 Dey at Behesht Reza cemetery after continuous searching by family.
Shot in the stomach with live ammunition by Islamic regime forces while being the family provider, taking care of his mother and sister

14-year-old boy under the care of Iran's Welfare Organization, shot by security forces during Iran's nationwide protests on January 8, 2026. Died after 10 days in hospital and multiple surgeries from his injuries. His body remained in a morgue for nearly 40 days before being secretly buried under security pressure. His gravestone was later vandalized.

27-year-old Kurdish citizen from Malekshahi in Ilam province. Shot in kidney, intestine and thigh by war bullets during protests on Thursday January 8th in Ilam. Died on Saturday January 17th due to deterioration of his physical condition from bullet wounds despite medical efforts. Security agencies demanded 500 million tomans and denial of burial rights in his hometown to return his body to family, or alternatively that family declare him a Basij force killed by protesters. Family was forced to declare him as government force to retrieve body due to financial inability, leading to local residents refusing burial in Malekshahi and boycotting funeral ceremony.

Disappeared on the night of 19 Dey 1404 while trying to deliver medical supplies to injured protesters in the Baharestan area of Isfahan following protest calls. After 10 days of uncertainty and contradictory information from authorities, his lifeless body was delivered to his family under strict security measures with threats. Burial ceremony was held under security surveillance with plainclothes agents present to expedite burial and prevent gatherings. Had recently obtained a crane driving license and planned to work officially.

One of two brothers killed during protests. Father of three young children, resident of Tehran. Shot directly by government forces during protests in Tehranpars area and fell into coma. While going to help his brother Hamid, was targeted with two bullets to the neck area. Died after 9 days on Friday 27 Dey. Security agencies demanded 1.5 billion tomans from family to return the body and forced them to sign a pledge declaring cause of death as 'brain stroke'. Also required family attendance at government rally in Revolution Square as condition for body return.

18-year-old youth team player for Delvarafraz Tehran. Shot directly with bullets by government suppressive forces in Afsarieh area of Tehran. A case was fabricated against him and his body was only handed over to the family on condition of being introduced as a Basij member. His body was forcibly buried by agents in section 42 alongside those killed known as 'martyrs of the 12-day war'.

18-year-old resident of Ghal'eh Hassan Khan. Shot by government forces with war bullets to the abdomen area on Thursday 18 Dey during popular protests, fell into coma, and died in hospital after 8 days due to severe damage to internal organs including stomach and kidneys.

Wrote a critical letter to Khamenei on January 7, 2026. His lifeless body was found beside a roadway in Marivan, Kurdistan province on January 16, 2026. His hands and feet were reportedly broken and signs of torture and beatings were visible throughout his body.
Killed in Shiraz on Rashd Shomali Street after arrest, torture, and beating, with a final shot to the throat

Kurdish citizen who was shot by government forces during popular protests in Khorramabad. He was hit by a direct bullet while on the balcony of his house on Razi Street. He was initially wounded and later died in the hospital. His body was buried in the cemetery of Ivandreh village in Kakaraza district.

Shot with war bullet on 19th of Dey, died after three days of resistance on 22nd of Dey. Was a 41-year-old young man who had gotten engaged about 6 months prior and had dreams of bringing his bride home
Injured on 19 Dey 1404 in Isfahan, died on 26 Dey from his injuries

38-year-old teacher and father of three 3-year-old triplets named Alma, Pasha and Gersha. Shot directly in the side with a bullet in Isfahan. His body was kept for six days and then delivered to the family under pressure and threats, with demands that they declare the cause of death as 'being a Basiji' and 'killed by terrorists'.
Shot in the chest by a plainclothes officer while trying to save three teenagers who were targeted by a sniper. Died after two surgeries due to embolism.
Shot on Friday, 19 Dey 1404 in Anzali (Naser Khosrow area) and died the following Wednesday. Authorities did not allow burial in Anzali, so he was buried in Ramsar instead.

Shot and killed during protests. Had been shot once before in 1401 (2022). Was from Malayer but lived in Marlik area of Karaj. Had been married for only 3 months. Family was pressured by security agencies and forced to choose between paying money or declaring him a 'martyr' to retrieve his body. Due to difficult financial situation, family was forced to agree to the regime's demand after waiting one week.

Kurdish citizen who fell into a coma after being shot with bullets and receiving multiple baton blows to the head during protests. Died after one week. Initially wounded on Thursday 18 Dey by government forces through shooting with war bullets and baton strikes. Body was buried on Friday 26 Dey.
Arrested on January 9, 2026 (19 Dey 1404) in Shahsavar and died on January 15, 2026 (25 Dey 1404)

Arrested by Islamic Republic forces on January 8, 2026 in Rasht. His lifeless body was returned to his family six days later. According to reports, his body showed signs of beating and torture, and he was killed by a gunshot to the head by Islamic Republic forces.

Kurdish citizen from Kermanshah who was severely wounded on January 7, 2026 during popular protests in Dizelabad, Kermanshah when government forces of the Islamic Republic shot him with more than 16 pellets to the head and face area. He was transferred to Taleghani Hospital in Kermanshah and after one week in intensive care, he died from the severity of his injuries. He was buried on January 14, 2026 in Tappeh Afshar village cemetery. Security agencies put severe pressure on his family to declare him as a member of the 'government Basij', but the family rejected this request. He was married with two young children and worked as a heavy machinery welder.

Resident of Sari, worked as a barista at Café Melo on Mahdieh Street. Targeted by gunfire from Iranian government forces at his workplace during protests. Succumbed to his injuries several days after being hospitalized in critical condition

Shot from behind with two bullets during protests on Friday January 9, 2026 in Sabashahr (suburb of Shahriar). One bullet hit above the heart and another hit his side. After being wounded, he was transported by pickup truck to three hospitals but none accepted him. Finally taken to a clinic where he died on January 14, 2026. Body was transferred to Kahrizak and burial ceremony held on January 15, 2026 in Sabashahr.

25 years old, resident of Tehran. Computer science student. Killed by direct gunfire from government forces during protests in Tehranpars area, shot in the head with a war bullet. His family was unaware of his fate for six days after his killing, and finally on Thursday 25 Dey they were contacted to go to forensic medicine for body identification.
Arrested on January 7, 2026 in Tehranpars, body returned to family on January 14, 2026 in Kahrizak with signs of severe torture, bullet in stomach, torn thigh muscle, bruising and burns on fingertips

17-year-old athlete from Tehran arrested on January 8, 2026 in the Narmak area. His lifeless body was delivered to his family a few days later. According to informed sources, signs of torture and jaw fractures were observed on the teenager's body, and the family was demanded payment for receiving the body. Reports indicate he was the second child of the family and was preparing as a wrestler to participate in provincial competitions. He was a motivated teenager with many dreams who joined the protests and lost his life under unclear circumstances after arrest.
From Boldaji, residing in Mobarakeh, Isfahan. On the 19th of Dey, shot in the leg and arrested. Tortured until the 24th, then executed with a final shot. Body was delivered on the 25th.
Mentioned during commemoration ceremony (chehellom). Described as a child of Iran and one who sacrificed for the homeland.

Abducted by regime's repressive forces and killed. Known for writing Mahsa Amini's name on a mountain during the Woman, Life, Freedom uprising's 40th day commemoration in 1401. Family was not allowed to bury him in Arak; his body was buried in the city of Astaneh.

Beaten with batons on Enqelab Street in Tehran on Thursday, 18 Dey (January 8, 2026) until his skull was shattered. Ambulance was prevented from reaching him. Was transported to Imam Khomeini Hospital by private car but died on 24 Dey (February 13, 2026).
Shot by Khamenei's forces during protests. Described as a decent, honorable child who loved his homeland and was always present in protests.

Killed by Khamenei's murderers in Amol. His body was stolen and his family was forced under threats and intimidation to introduce him as a Basiji member. Everyone in Amol who knew Iliya knows he hated this regime.

Shot with direct gunfire to the head area at close range by Islamic Republic of Iran regime agents during nationwide protests in Dey month. The shooting was carried out deliberately at Shahrbanai Square in Babolsar city. He was the only child of his family and was killed on the spot.

Shot by Islamic Republic forces on Zahedi Street in Tehranpars on January 8, 2026, and taken to hospital. Death certificate issued on January 13 with false cause listed as 'cardiac arrest due to electrocution'. Body identified after more than 20 days through fingerprinting and family notified. Body delivered in Kahrizak with severe signs of beating (body bruising and broken nose) under security pressure and commitments. Family threatened with judicial subpoena and forced burial in section 329 of Behesht Zahra cemetery.

Shot directly by Islamic Republic agents on Zahedi Street in Tehranpars on January 8th and transferred to hospital. After more than 20 days, his identity was confirmed through fingerprinting. His body was delivered to family from Kahrizak under pressure and threats, showing severe signs of beatings, bruising, broken nose, and torn chest. Death certificate falsely listed cause as 'cardiac arrest due to electrocution'.
Kurdish Yarsan from Zardeh Dalahu, killed by direct shooting by military forces during popular protests

Shot with war bullets by government criminals in the liver and spleen area during Iran's national uprising on 18 Dey (2026-01-08). Severely injured and transferred to hospital. After 3 days of enduring pain and deprivation of safe medical care, died on the path to homeland freedom. Example of direct shooting, targeted killing and abandoning youth in death cycle.

Shot and killed along with Hadi Ghanbarpour at a checkpoint 5 kilometers from Jonghan city. Their car was inspected at the checkpoint, and when they got back in to leave, they were shot from 5-10 meters away with dozens of war bullets. Aligholi was severely injured by dozens of bullets and pellets to his chest, head, neck, face and spinal cord. After being hospitalized for several days, he was abducted from the hospital at midnight by regime security forces accompanied by clerics. His body was returned to his family after he died. The regime attempted to present him as a 'martyr of the Islamic Republic' but this was rejected by the people of Jonghan.

Shot and killed along with Aligholi Mokhtari at a checkpoint 5 kilometers from Jonghan city. Their car was inspected at the checkpoint, and when they got back in to leave, they were shot from 5-10 meters away with dozens of war bullets by IRGC suppression and intelligence forces. The regime attempted to present him as a 'martyr of the Islamic Republic' but this was rejected by the people of Jonghan. Both bodies were buried under strict security measures with families forced to give commitments not to publicize the matter.

33 years old, with associate degree in petrochemical industries, from Abadan. Shot by government forces during people's protests on 22 Dey 1404, targeted in the neck and leg area. Body has not been returned to family yet.

Shot with pellet gun by government forces at close range while helping an injured person at Sharifabad intersection in Shiraz. More than 80 pellets were extracted from his right kidney. The pellet hit his right kidney which had to be removed, he underwent surgery twice with part of his intestines also removed, and his left kidney failed. He was a truck driver who recently owned a spare parts shop. Family was not allowed to see the wound site, only his face, and his funeral was held under restrictions with four security forces present.
Mother of a teenage daughter, arrested and killed by intelligence forces in Yaftabad area of Tehran
31-year-old powerlifting champion who was shot and killed on Monday 22 Dey 1404 in Semnan province. The young protester lost his life after being struck by a bullet to the head area.

Shot in front of Basij base in Rudbar. Was the only son of his family, loved sports, and had opened a small mobile phone repair shop. His body was so tall that the coffin was too small and his legs stuck out. Authorities initially withheld his body and when they returned it, forced his family to claim it was the work of terrorists. Family was threatened that if they told the truth, his body would be stolen from the grave.

Shot by forces of the Islamic Republic regime. According to his niece, he was shot directly while participating in protests. He was described as a healthy man who went to the mountains and was mindful of his diet, had 5 grandchildren, and was in the streets during the protests when he was killed.

Shot with a war bullet during suppression of popular protests in Mehrshahr area of Karaj around 8:30 PM on Friday. According to eyewitness accounts, he was targeted in the abdomen area and the bullet exited through his back. Plainclothes agents had created tension among the crowd and thrown stones at shop shutters before security forces fired tear gas and threw grenades. At least two people were shot during the incident.
Shot by direct gunfire from Iranian government forces on the evening of Sunday, January 11, 2026 during December protests

Shot by live ammunition (گلوله جنگی) while working in Tehran Bazaar. His body was released to family only after payment of 1.6 billion Tomans (~$24,000 USD). Funeral was held under tight security measures with only immediate family allowed. He had previously been wounded by pellet rounds during the 2022 protests.
Detained during protests. Listed in HRANA's 'Identified Detainees' section of The Crimson Winter report. Date of arrest: January 2, 2026 (1404/10/13). Current status: Unknown. Original source: HRANA.

3-year-old child who died from injuries sustained from direct gunfire by government suppressive forces during protests. Was hit by a bullet during clashes between government forces and protesters on January 9, 2026, was wounded and fell into a coma before dying. Body was buried on January 14, 2026 in the cemetery of Shadab village in Neyshabur. Government institutions blamed protesters for her death.

Medical student at Qazvin University of Medical Sciences. Shot with three war bullets by security forces. First bullet hit her abdomen, second bullet hit her heart, third bullet hit her abdomen again. Three security agents stood over her preventing anyone from helping her. Her body was found covered in blood. Security forces confiscated the surveillance video. Family was forced to pay 600 million tomans (200 million per bullet) to receive her body after 6 days. Forced to claim she died in a terrorist attack. Not allowed to bury her in her hometown Takestan, buried in Qazvin without public attendance.

Baloch citizen shot directly in the neck by government forces during popular protests in Neyshabur. Suffered severe spinal cord injury and spinal cord severance. Died after three days in ICU. Body was returned to family after three days for 300 million tomans.
Citizen of Talesh from Makelovan area of Fouman, worked as tire seller in Tehran, shot directly by forces of the Islamic Republic of Iran in Nezam-Abad area of Tehran
Killed during killed protester citizens protests. HRANA verification: Confirmed. Original source: Kurdpa.
Construction worker (gypsum worker). Killed in Shiraz, Adelabad neighborhood, Qahramanan Square. Funeral was held only with close family members and security presence.

Bakhtiari ethnicity. Killed by proxy forces of the Islamic Republic (Iraqi forces) during recent protests. He defended himself and people against repression by Iraqi terrorist forces, confronted them and neutralized one of them, but terrorists from Hashd al-Shaabi fired seven bullets into his chest

Shot with bullets in the Zia Abri area of Rasht city. After his death, security agencies prevented any public ceremonies for him. His family was pressured and forced to sign a written commitment to introduce his body as a 'Basiji'. His body was buried in the Nasirolmahalle mosque in Shaft without public ceremonies. He was a 52-year-old Gilak citizen from Shaft.
Killed during killed protester citizens protests. HRANA verification: Confirmed. Original source: IRNA – Office of the President.

42-year-old father of three young children, owner of a furniture factory, resident of Tehran. On 18 Dey 1404 during popular protests in Tehran Pars neighborhood, he was severely injured by a bullet to the head. His brother sat beside him to help when he was targeted by two bullets. Both brothers were taken to hospital. Hamid died after 3 days of unconsciousness. Security agencies demanded 1.5 billion tomans from the family to hand over the bodies of the two brothers and forced them to sign a commitment stating the cause of death was 'brain stroke'.
Shot by direct gunfire from Iranian government forces on the evening of Sunday, January 11, 2026 during December protests

Shot from behind with a war bullet during Islamic Republic forces' suppression in Arak city. Originally from Khomein district. His body was buried on Sunday 21 Dey in his birthplace Khomein under severe security conditions.
Killed during protests in Tehran. Worked at a car showroom in Abbas Abad, Tehran.
Died in Kahrizsang area of Najafabad, Isfahan

Shot by Islamic Republic agents on January 8, 2026 with a Kalashnikov rifle bullet to his right side, then shot with a shotgun to his thigh. He sustained severe injuries and died on the evening of January 9, 2026 due to the severity of his wounds.
Shot by direct gunfire from Iranian government forces on the evening of Sunday, January 11, 2026 during December protests
Injured on January 8, 2026 in Tehran and died on January 11, 2027

Shot by Basij forces while passing by a Basij base with her sister on Dolat Street. They were going to a pharmacy when a gunman from the base suddenly aimed through the car window and shot her.
