Glass shattered on her sister's head and eyes. Her sister screamed 'Narges, go!' before sudden silence filled the street with the smell of gunpowder and narcissus flowers. She was described as stubborn and kind, studying to become a doctor, and always helped a little flower-selling girl at intersections.

Shot in the stomach with pellet bullets by regime mercenaries at Kakh Square in Gorgan on January 8, 2026. Doctors performed surgery but she could not survive and died on January 11. She was a sports science graduate, athlete, and physiotherapist. Also known as Fatemeh.
Killed during killed protester citizens protests. HRANA verification: Confirmed. Original source: HRANA.
Buried on 21 Dey, birthday was 22 Dey, died at approximately 20 years old
Powerlifting champion from Shahroud, killed during protests in the city after being struck by live ammunition to the head
Killed by IRGC gunfire on his birthday. He had called his friends to invite them to his birthday celebration.

Gilaki citizen from Bandar Anzali, arrested by security forces in Rasht on 19 Dey 1404. Released after two days of detention with extensive bruising throughout her body. Hours after release, her physical condition deteriorated with severe nausea. She died while her family was attempting to transport her to Bandar Anzali hospital.

Shot with pellets by IRGC forces at 8:30 PM on Basirat Street in Fooladshahr, Isfahan. He was alone when a motorcycle stopped beside him. Out of fear, he sat on the ground, pulled his hood over his head and said 'Please, for God's sake, don't hit me.' The forces placed their weapon on his back and shot him with pellets. More than 50 pellets entered his body, hitting his spinal cord and heart. His body was handed over to his family on the 26th and buried under security measures.

Shot at close range with pellet ammunition by forces of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) on Basirat Street in Fuladshahr while he was alone. At least 50 pellets penetrated his body, causing catastrophic injuries to his spinal cord and heart. His body was returned to his family on January 16, 2026, and he was buried under heavy security measures, with authorities imposing strict restrictions on funeral and mourning ceremonies.

Approximately 54-year-old father of three children from Tehran, killed by direct gunfire from Iranian government forces during popular protests in District 15 of Tehran. Died after being hit by a Kalashnikov bullet fired by government forces.
Shot in the head and chest by suppressive forces during people's protests. Married with three children, originally from Ardabil, residing in Parand.
Killed during non-participating civilians protests. HRANA verification: Confirmed. Original source: HRANA.

Turkish citizen, resident of Ramsar, killed by Islamic Republic of Iran government forces while driving. He encountered a security checkpoint, failed to stop when ordered, and his vehicle was shot at with gunfire, resulting in his death. The city of Ramsar was under heavy security control at the time of the incident.

Shot by suppression forces on Friday January 9, 2026 with two bullets - one in the thigh and one in the stomach. Died two days later on January 11, 2026 at Imam Hussein Hospital in Tehran. Described as a freedom-loving woman whose name and photo had not appeared in any lists, prompting calls to preserve her memory.
37-year-old resident of Shahreza, shot by direct fire from government forces during popular protests on 19 Dey 1404
47-year-old worker, father's name Mir Ali, company worker/supervisor of two children and elderly mother, died during the early hours of the call on the eighteenth (18 Dey)
64-year-old citizen wounded in the leg by direct war bullet from suppressive forces. His body was discovered in Shiraz and buried in Bajgah village.
Died on 19 Dey 1404 in Tehran
Shot directly by government suppressive forces while on foot during people's protests in Nazi-Abad, Behesht Square

19-year-old student from the Bakhtiari tribe, specifically from Dorki clan, Zarasound sub-tribe. Shot by government forces in Isfahan. Family kept his body on ice overnight fearing it would be taken by authorities. Buried in Dorak Shapouri village (Anari) in Chaharmahal and Bakhtiari province on January 10, 2026. Funeral held at 5 AM under heavy security presence with widespread government agents present.
Listed in Iran International Memorial

Native of Quchan but resident of Mashhad. Shot by IRGC forces during the national revolution at Student Bridge (Pol-e Daneshjou) in Mashhad alongside his friend Shayan Bodaghi. Both were friends who sacrificed their lives for the homeland. Buried in Quchan under security measures.

Gilak citizen from Chaboksar, born in 1361 (1982), father of a 17-year-old son and 13-year-old daughter. Shot in the back with war bullets by Islamic Republic of Iran government forces during popular protests in Chaboksar on 19 Dey 1404 (January 8, 2026). After enduring severe injuries for several days, he died on 28 Dey 1404

Shot during popular protests on 19 Dey 1404 in Isfahan by direct fire from security forces. He was hit by two bullets from behind on Zeinabiyeh Street and was transferred to Gharzi Hospital where he died due to severe injuries and punctured lung. He had been married for less than two years.

Shot on 18 Dey in Mahdasht, Karaj, while his daughter Ailar was beside him. Died from severe bleeding and thirst in his final moments. His daughter had gone to bring him water after he said he was thirsty.

Shot with war bullets while returning from work on the evening of his birthday. Initially wounded by gunshot to the thigh area, then killed by security forces with finishing shots. Father of a 4-year-old daughter. Security forces used war bullets against protesters who had no weapons and showed no signs of armed conflict.
Father of two children, shot by Islamic Republic forces in front of the governor's office in Homayounshahr (Khomaini-shahr) during the Iranian national revolution

26-year-old law graduate student from Mahmoudabad, Mazandaran. During the national revolution, he suffered severe injuries after body search and beating with batons. He was transferred to hospital but died due to the severity of his injuries. Family was forced to hold funeral and mourning ceremonies in a limited, private manner due to security pressures.

Shot in the chest by Islamic Republic suppressive forces in Saba Shahr area of Shahriyar during nationwide protests. Companions sought medical help at hospitals in Shahriyar and Karaj for 3 hours but hospitals refused to admit him. Suppressive forces also shot at their vehicle during this time. He died due to severity of injuries and lack of medical care. Father of a 10-year-old daughter and 7-year-old son.
Engineer, shot in the throat and hit in the head with a war projectile during protests, died as a martyr for the homeland

Shot during peaceful protest in Andisheh Township, Phase 3, Ferdowsi Street near Rudaki intersection. Direct gunfire from behind and from an unknown location hit his back and side around 8:20 PM while he was attending a protest gathering with his daughter. Despite severe bleeding and pain, he remained conscious and speaking. He was transported to hospital by his daughter and several young people but died after approximately two hours due to severe blood loss. His body was transferred to Kahrizak and identified by his son and close relatives among thousands of bodies two days later. Buried in Behesht Zahra cemetery.
Shot by Islamic Republic forces in Narmak, Tehran on January 8, 2026, hit in the side and intestine area. Died on January 17, 2026 at Milad Hospital in Tehran from his injuries.

25-year-old microbiology graduate student at Ferdowsi University of Mashhad. Two accounts exist of his death: first account suggests he was killed on the night of 20 Dey month with body returned to family after ten days; second account indicates he was first arrested and after about a week his family was called to collect his body
Killed during killed protester citizens protests. HRANA verification: Confirmed. Original source: IRNA – Office of the President.
Died in Isfahan during the protests
Shot directly in the stomach area during protests in Fardis, Karaj. Killed for standing with the people and Iran's national uprising.

Turkmen citizen of Gonbad-e Kavus, shot directly by Islamic Republic of Iran government forces on Sarabi Street during protests in the city. His body was delivered to the family after several days at 6 AM. Before the body was delivered, the family was repeatedly threatened by security institutions to 'not make trouble'. Two security agents accompanied and controlled the family inside the morgue. The body was transferred directly to the cemetery and the family only had a few seconds to see his body. The burial ceremony was conducted without public rituals and under security pressures at Gonbad-e Kavus cemetery.
Died on the night of 20 Dey in Dorcheh, Isfahan. Was 29 years old and his birthday was one week later.

20-year-old electronics engineering student at Azad University who was shot in the leg during popular protests in the Nazi Abad area of Tehran on Thursday evening January 8, 2026. After being shot, he took shelter in a residential parking garage for about 3 hours due to fear of continued clashes until the area calmed down. He was first taken to Amir al-Momenin Hospital in Javadiyeh but was refused admission due to lack of facilities. He was then transferred by ambulance to Shariati Hospital where he underwent surgery. His mother, Soraya Shams-Zare (retired from Welfare Organization), reported that the surgery was successful, but hours later medical staff announced that he had not regained consciousness and had died. His body was transferred to Kahrizak Legal Medicine after death. He was from a family with a history of sacrifice - his mother is the sister of Hojjatollah Shams-Zare, who was killed in the Iran-Iraq War.
Shot directly in the chest with internal bleeding by suppressors during the night

22-year-old champion athlete in Kyokushin karate, member of Iran's national team (2022), participant in Asian championships in Kazakhstan, official Kyokushin karate referee. Shot in the heart with a war bullet by Islamic Republic suppression forces at 9:30 PM while trying to save an injured person in Sirjan city.

Artesh First Lieutenant shot and killed 'while on mission' in Khavaran neighbourhood, eastern Tehran. Iranian state media reported his death and US-based human rights activists acknowledged his death on 20 January.

Shot in the heart during protests. On the night of 18 Dey, he came home with pellet marks on his clothes and his mother asked him not to go to the streets anymore, but Amirali went out again on Friday night and his body was delivered to his mother with a bullet wound to his heart.

Orphan raised by grandmother with worker's wages, shot directly by security forces near the police station in Rostamkola, Behshahr County, Mazandaran Province. Body returned to family after several days with severe restrictions

Shot with 3 military bullets in hand and stomach during protests in Nazi Abad, Tehran on the night of 18 Dey. Died at 7 AM the next morning (Friday) at Pakdasht Hospital. Husband and father of a 4-year-old daughter. Orphaned from childhood, grew up without parents.
Killed one day after his birthday. His family reached out to friends saying 'they killed my brother' but they don't know where to report it to keep his name alive.
Shot in the eye with a bullet. Originally from Tuyserkan, killed in Eslamshahr, Tehran.

13-year-old child from Norabad Delfan, shot in the back with a war bullet by military-security forces of the Islamic Republic during popular protests in Qaemiyeh neighborhood, Eslamshahr, Tehran province. Security institutions pressured the family to introduce him as a Basij member in exchange for handing over the body, but the family refused this demand. His body was handed over to the family after four days and payment of one billion tomans, and was buried under pressure from security institutions in Dehshad cemetery

16-year-old teenager shot with live ammunition in the upper chest/heart area outside Jaber Ansari Street metro station in Isfahan. He was severely wounded and died after being transferred to Dr. Gharazi Hospital in Isfahan. His body was returned to his family about a week later. Shot directly with war weapons targeting vital areas of the body including head and heart by security forces, demonstrating clear intent to kill.
Shot with a barrage of bullets while inside his personal vehicle around midnight. Gilak citizen from Khoshkbijar. Killed on the spot due to severity of injuries.

Graduate student in IT at Azad University Tehran, killed by suppressive forces with multiple pellet shots and knife wounds; combination of bullets and blade used to deliberately kill an educated young man

University student at the University of Art and resident of Tehran's Majidieh neighborhood, killed during protests in the capital after being shot by Iranian forces. Worked as a life-skills coach and was active in the field of healing and wellness.

Shot directly in the back of the head by Islamic Republic agents during protests in Nazi-Abad neighborhood of Tehran. He was a goldsmith and had called his relatives shortly before his death to say goodbye. His burial ceremony was held on 22 Dey at Behesht-e Zahra cemetery.
Also known as Abdeh Vand, from Joyabad neighborhood, martyred during protests
Killed during killed protester citizens protests. HRANA verification: Confirmed. Original source: HRANA.

19-year-old physical education student shot and killed by Islamic Republic forces in Sayghalan Square in Rasht. He had joined street protests following a call by Crown Prince Reza Pahlavi. Shot from behind in the head area and died before being transported to medical center. His body was handed over to his family three days later, and his burial ceremony was held under security conditions at 10 PM. Family was asked to refrain from holding any public ceremonies or publicizing the incident.

Born April 6, 1997 (16 Farvardin 1376). Had an associate degree in management and worked at his father's cookery. Shot in the head with a war bullet while protecting his mother and two sisters during protests. He had taken them to a side alley when security and military forces began direct shooting at people. When he looked out to see if officers were following them, he was shot in the head. Died in the arms of his mother and two sisters. Family was forced to pay one billion tomans to retrieve his body.

Shot in the throat while with friends in Fardis, Karaj. Taken to hospital but was not admitted due to lack of capacity and died while being transferred to another hospital. Originally from west Tehran.

Shot with direct fire by suppressive forces during popular protests in Fardis, Karaj. Hit by war bullet in the temple area. Security institutions pressured his family to bury his body in Mahdasht, Alborz province.

Shot with a war bullet in Elahiyeh neighborhood of Kermanshah on 19 Dey. Family was severely pressured and threatened not to speak about it. No ceremonies were allowed at home or during burial. The mosque mentioned for the ceremony was filled with plainclothes security forces. Funeral announcements were immediately torn down from walls.

Shot in the throat in front of his house in Fooladshahr. Originally from Ramhormoz. His body remained in his mother's arms until morning when authorities took it. Authorities demanded one billion in ransom for the body and said the family must declare he was a Basiji member.

Well-known farmer in Torbat-e Jam, father of four children with one grandchild, shot directly from behind in the head by Islamic Republic forces. Security agencies later contacted his family calling it 'human error' and offered to declare him a 'martyr' but family rejected this. Part of wider suppression in Torbat-e Jam where over 100 people were killed including children, with direct shooting at unarmed citizens in the streets.

Shot directly in the neck artery by Islamic Republic forces while opening her door to rescue two young men who were being beaten in the alley. Her body was buried after 4 days under security measures without permission for viewing, after obtaining commitments from the family. Mother of two children aged 8 and 16. Writer and literature specialist who wrote short stories and cared about Iran and children. According to those around her, she had a very attractive writing style and was brave and fearless.
57-year-old hero who was sheltering protesters in his home in Isfahan. He went outside to see if anyone was left alone to bring them to his house, and was shot while doing so.

Shot directly in the heart by Islamic Republic suppression forces. His body was buried in Aghozban village. He was a bodybuilding athlete and crane operator; described as a kind, knowledgeable, brave and patriotic young man who stood for Iran's freedom and dignity.
Shot on Saheb-Zaman street during national uprising. Was from the Yarsan religious minority community from Qaleh Hassan Khan.
Victim documented in Iran International Memorial

Young worker, renter, father of three children. Went to Baharestan, Isfahan for work to earn honest living for better life. Was shot and killed. His body was buried at night in Izeh with security measures, as if they wanted to extinguish his name with darkness.
48-year-old man from Mashhad. Located on Emamaat Boulevard in Razavi Khorasan province.
Died on 19 Dey 1404 in Arak
Shot in the heart with a war bullet by the Islamic Republic regime in Khomeini Square in Malayer. Family was forced to pay 400 million [tomans/rials] to retrieve his body.

Armenian citizen living in Majidieh neighborhood of Tehran, killed by direct gunfire from Iranian government suppressive forces during protests in Narmak neighborhood. Part of Armenia community which forms the largest group of Christians in Iran.

Shot directly in the heart with a bullet on Ahmadabad Street in Mashhad. She was a married mother of a 5-year-old child. Her body was buried several days after her killing at Behesht-e Reza cemetery in Mashhad, around the time of her birthday anniversary.
Shot with direct bullet to the heart area by forces of the Islamic Republic of Iran government during protests. Gilaki citizen from Lashtnesha.

Shot in the head with pellet bullets during protests in Rasht. Gilaki citizen from Shaft. Family was pressured by security agencies to sign a commitment letter stating 'fall from height' as the cause of death in the hospital report before the body was released. Family was forced to hold burial and mourning ceremonies in complete silence without public ceremonies.

Follower of Yarsani faith, shot with pellet gun on January 8 during protests, returned to streets on January 9 and was wounded in the leg, then killed with a finishing shot by security forces. He had sent text messages to his wife and daughter during the protests that were not delivered due to phone network outages. In his message to his wife he wrote: 'If something happens to me, don't be afraid, stand like a lion.' In his final moments he wrote to his daughter: 'I love you very much.'

21-year-old Baloch citizen killed during popular protests in Bandar Abbas by direct shooting of war bullet by military and security forces to the head area. Son of Mohammad Youssef, from Zahedan and resident of Bandar Abbas. Body was delivered to family from Khalij Fars Hospital and buried on 22 Dey (January 12) in Mohammad Rasool Allah cemetery in Zahedan. This is the third Baloch victim in the nationwide 1404 protests.

Shot in the head with live ammunition by government forces during protests. Also known as Kashani, a Baloch man originally from Zahedan and resident of Bandar Abbas. Killed on the spot during Saturday night protests. Body received by family from Persian Gulf Hospital, transferred to Zahedan and buried on January 12, 2026 at Mohammad Rasulollah Cemetery.

Shot with pellets in the back of his shoulder in Fallah district of Tehran, arrested, then killed with a fatal shot under his chin. The bullet passed through his left eye and exited from the left side of his head. Father of a 5-year-old daughter.
Death on January 10, 2026 in Mobarakeh, Isfahan during the 2025-2026 Iranian protests
Died on 20 Dey 1404 in Shahinshahr, Isfahan. Part of numbered list (#1685) of victims being memorialized.

PhD student and mathematics elite who was shot by Islamic Republic forces on the night of January 8, 2026 during suppression of popular protests. He was severely wounded and hospitalized for several days before dying from his injuries. Previously beaten severely during Green Movement protests in Shiraz to the point of near death. Had been under security pressure for years including detention, expulsion from university, and systematic educational and professional restrictions. According to his cousin, he was killed with a shot to the heart while in Islamic Republic custody.
Killed during popular protests in Mobarakeh city, Isfahan province by direct gunfire from government suppressive forces.

Former footballer and football referee from Delijan, killed during protests in the Fardis area of Karaj by the criminal forces of the Islamic Republic. His body was laid to rest in his hometown of Delijan.

Killed during popular protests in Mobarakeh city, Isfahan province by direct gunfire from government suppressive forces.
Shot during protests on Aram Boulevard in Mehrshahr, Karaj. Family was not permitted to hold a public burial.
34-year-old patriotic young man shot directly by Islamic Republic terrorists in the waist and heart on the 19th of Dey month in Dezful
University professor and teacher. Shot in the heart by Islamic Republic agents at Hasht-Behesht intersection in Isfahan. His body was found by his family after five days of searching.
Conscripted soldier and hairdresser, killed by direct gunfire from Islamic Republic regime suppression forces in the Nodeh area and Meraj Square on Tous Boulevard in Mashhad

Shot directly by Islamic Republic agents in the side and abdomen on the night of 18 Dey, hospitalized for two days at Kashani Hospital in Shahr-e Kord before dying. His burial ceremony was not permitted and he was buried at night at 2 AM in Farkhshar.

Shot in the stomach by Islamic Republic security forces bullets in the city of Shazand. His burial was conducted at 5 AM under government threat and coercion, without the presence or knowledge of the people
Victim documented in Iran International Memorial from Meshkin Dasht, Karaj

Shot from behind with direct military fire (confirmed Kalashnikov) in Pasdaran neighborhood on January 7, 2026. Bullet exited from his left eye. Family arranged transfer to private hospital where neurosurgeons confirmed he could be saved, but hospital staff administered an injection that caused cardiac arrest within an hour. Body was held hostage by authorities who demanded payment of 1.2 billion tomans ($8,900) before releasing it to family. Funeral held under heavy security with family threatened against continuing mourning.

Shot directly by Iranian Islamic Republic government forces in Jamaran neighborhood of Rasht, near a Basij base. The bullet hit his head and the incident occurred in front of his brother's eyes. He was a Gilaki citizen from Rasht.

Shot and killed during national revolution protests in Nazi Abad neighborhood of Tehran. Hit by 8 war bullets, with two bullets fired directly to the head. Was an accounting student, had recently completed military service, and worked as a CNC operator in an industrial complex. Was the youngest child in his family. Body was buried in Behesht-e Zahra cemetery in Tehran.
31-year-old programmer and composer who had recently married. Shot and killed by regime forces at Chahar Rah Rashid intersection in Tehran.
Died in Vavan, Eslamshahr

Shot and killed with three bullets during protests in front of the Shahroud governor's office. Security forces fired war bullets from the top of the governor's building at the crowd. He had not been outside on January 8th and was unaware of how chaotic things had become, but went out to the street on January 9th.

Captain Mahmoud Ghasemi was a well-known captain of Bushehr boats and originally from Bandar Boalakhir. He was killed on the 18th of Dey month. His body was retrieved from the morgue of Ghaem Hospital of Sepah Bushehr after 4 days with threats and intimidation, and was buried in his birthplace. Mahmoud Ghasemi had willed to be remembered as the Lion of Tangassir. He had two daughters.

Shot by Islamic Republic suppression forces in the Seh Rah-e Simin area of Isfahan. According to reports, he was hit from very close range with war bullets in the side and neck area and died due to the severity of his injuries.

64-year-old mother from Malayer who came to the streets on Friday 19 Dey to stand in solidarity with the youth of her country. Shot by Islamic Republic terrorists. According to others, she had also participated in protests on Thursday and sheltered many young people who had been shot in her home.
Killed during popular protests in Mobarakeh city, Isfahan province by direct gunfire from government suppressive forces.
Details about circumstances of death not provided in the text
Died in Yaft Abad, Shams Abad neighborhood. Local children requested that his memory not be forgotten.
Shot during mass execution in a dead-end alley in Parand, Tehran. 21 people were trapped in the alley and all were gunned down except one survivor. Originally from Boroujerd and buried there.
Father of children, wounded by war bullet on Thursday 18 Dey in Tehran, died on 20 Dey becoming a martyr for the homeland

Shot with at least 50 pellets at close range by regime suppression forces in Sattar Khan neighborhood while leaving her house to join her husband during national uprising. The extensive pellet wounds caused severe internal bleeding and she fell on the sidewalk and died.
Ceramic artist, mother of a 14-year-old son. Shot by Islamic Republic agents with pellet bullets to her heart and lungs in Punak area of Tehran. Body was buried in Torbat-e Heydarieh.
Described as 'child of Iran and soul sacrificed for the homeland'

Shot directly with a war bullet to the liver area by government forces. 48-year-old head of household, mother of two children and caregiver to elderly parents. After her killing, severe pressure was exerted on the family to declare her as a 'martyr' and accept the government's official narrative. Her body was transported to northern Iran for burial under intense security pressure with very limited attendees. The mosque door was not allowed to be opened for her ceremony, despite her previous extensive financial contributions to that mosque. Her body showed suspicious conditions - her abdomen was completely cut and stitched, but no signs of stitching or repair were visible at the bullet impact site, raising serious doubts about possible unauthorized organ removal.

Died after his body was found following a six-day search. Authorities demanded 200 million [tomans/rials] from his father in exchange for returning his son's body and permission to bury him in the martyrs' section of the cemetery. His father, who has years of experience with the opposition front, refused with dignity, saying his son gave his life for the homeland and was not a mercenary killer.
Shot by direct fire from government suppressive forces during people's protests in Baharestan
Killed by direct gunfire from government forces during protests in Arak on the evening of Friday, January 10.
Killed during recent protests. Described as a childhood, adolescent, and youth friend and playmate of the tweet author.

21-year-old civil engineering student shot with war bullet in the side during protests on 22 Bahman Street in Shahrood. He was with his brother during the national uprising when shooting began. Shot with war bullets while protesting on Saheb road in Shushtar. He took to the streets for freedom and human dignity, raised his voice to protest the conditions of his country, and was killed by order of Khamenei while in his prime, less than three decades old.

Shot from behind, appearing to suffer spinal cord injury and unable to walk. Companions hid him under a car for an hour to prevent security forces from delivering a fatal shot. Engineer and English translator killed by live ammunition fired by security forces during protest crackdown in the vicinity of Sharafat Park (Azadegan area). Shot while unarmed civilian during 'Bloody Friday' of January 2026. State used military-grade weaponry in high-density urban area.
Died in Karaj on 18 Dey 1404. Listed in a memorial account that remembers victims by name.

Shot directly in the side area, then executed with a gunshot to the forehead. Family found his body after several searches and during identification observed signs of the execution shot to the head. 35-year-old gym owner and resident of Saveh, shot by suppressive forces during popular protests on the evening of 19 Dey 1404. In a video released of him, he said 'My Iran is more precious than my body'

School principal of Niknam High School in Fardis, Karaj. Shot directly by Islamic Republic terrorists while serving his educational role in society. Killed not on a battlefield but in the heart of society, in the path of education and building the future of generations.

Shot with pellet gun on January 11, 2026. Was hospitalized at Imam Hussein Hospital in Tehran for several days before dying on January 19. Resident of Tehran, originally from Sabzevar. Was a powerlifting referee and athlete.

Shot by Islamic Republic suppression agents in Baharestan area of Isfahan. First shot in right leg, fell wounded, taken by ambulance to Alzahra Hospital Isfahan. Shot in the chest area by police officers from Mashiriyeh police station in the Mashiriyeh neighborhood. Shot by a sniper of Islamic Republic forces in the head area in Jahan Nama neighborhood of Karaj near his residence while going to the corner of the street to buy food for his daughters. Shot directly by regime mercenaries during national uprising in Iran for the crime of being present in the street and standing with the people. Trading activist killed by direct gunfire while unarmed.

Shot during protests in Sadeghieh area of Tehran. After being hit by a bullet, he managed to hide in a park but died due to severe bleeding. 30-year-old resident of Islamshahr, Tehran. Severely injured during popular protests by direct shooting from government forces with war bullets, then died in hospital due to severity of injuries. Body has not yet been returned to family.
Shot and killed by regime suppression forces. In his last video, he begged President Trump not to let them kill protesters. He spoke up for freedom and was answered with bullets.

Shot with shotgun pellets in the Jaber Ansari neighborhood of Isfahan during the Lion and Sun Revolution of Iran. Multiple pellets hit her waist and heart area, and she died at the scene. Finally his body was found and with great difficulty transferred to their city. There the family faced threats from IRGC forces and were asked to conduct the burial ceremony in silence and at night. Despite these pressures, there is still concern about desecration of his grave, so guards are kept at Alireza's grave from night until morning for fear that the body of this innocent teenager might be desecrated. Shot by IRGC forces on Shiroudi Boulevard in front of Refah Bank. Bullet struck his left side, causing ruptured spleen and severe internal bleeding. Despite hospital being only 500 meters away, military forces prevented immediate transfer. Was first taken to Shiroud, then by ambulance to Ramsar Hospital. Died after approximately 3 hours, denied basic medical care including IV. 27 years old, single, worked as hairdresser, owned shop, car, motorcycle, and newly completed home.

Shot with war bullets by security forces at Helal Ahmar intersection in Izeh city. The bullet hit his shoulder and he died hours later due to heavy bleeding. Security forces transferred his body to Ahvaz and forced his family to remain silent. Against the family's wishes, they imposed a martyrdom narrative and forced the family to bury him in the martyrs' section under strict security measures without holding tribal ceremonies. The family did not use the word 'martyr' in their obituary notice.
Young man from Shahreza who died, father held farewell ceremony with traditional reversed/upside-down musical instruments as per Lor and Qashqai tribal mourning customs for fallen heroes
Lor from Mamasani, killed when security forces opened fire on his personal car
23-year-old Gilaki citizen from Ishka village in Lahijan district, killed by Islamic Republic of Iran government forces in the evening on Thursday in the area of Lahijan National Garden

Shot in the stomach with a war bullet while helping injured protesters. The bullet caused serious damage to his intestines, stomach, and large parts of his abdomen. He was targeted while trying to help the wounded during the protests.
32-year-old computer industry worker shot and killed by the Islamic terrorist regime in Haft-houz. A young man who wanted to live, worked, and had a future, but received bullets in response.
25-year-old doctoral student who sacrificed his life for his homeland, died exactly one week before receiving his study visa
Killed while helping injured people in Vakil-Abad area during protests. From Khorasan province.

Shot with two war bullets to his leg and heart on 18th Dey in Dowlat Abad (Tehran). Family was forced under threat to say he was a Basij member. Authorities took photos and videos from the family, then later removed banners and said 'Do you believe he was a Basij?! He is one of the rioters and you have no right to mourn!' Bodies were piled on top of each other in one room at Dowlat Abad clinic to hide the number of killed. Father and brother searched for 12 hours at Kahrizak. Bodies were scattered like watermelons, naked boys and girls, had to be placed one by one to see those underneath due to the large number of deaths.

Shot by Islamic Republic agents in Haft Howz Square, Tehran. He was 17 years old and the only child of his family. His family refused the Islamic Republic agents' financial offer of several billion tomans and monthly salary to declare Pouya a 'government martyr'.

Killed during protests on 18th of Dey month. His body was found among other victims of Iran's national revolution. Brother of the tweet author Payam Fotouhiyehpour, went missing on Wednesday when communication with outside the country became available, family and friends searched everywhere but found no trace until Tuesday when they were informed his body was found among the martyrs.

Shot with direct gunfire by snipers of Islamic Republic agents in Falah neighborhood of Tehran. His body was delivered to family with delay and due to severity of injuries, identification was only possible through his clothes. Family is under pressure to accept the Islamic Republic's narrative.
Shot with bullet
29-year-old citizen of Esfarayen killed by direct fire of three bullets from government forces to jaw, head and chest area during popular protests in police station compound.

Kurdish Kurmanji youth from Esfarayen, North Khorasan province. Shot by Islamic Republic suppression forces with war bullets to the mouth and chest area during popular protests. Lost his father in childhood and had one brother; their mother raised the two children alone.

Shot in the head with a war bullet at Abuzar intersection, in front of a bank in Nahavand. Body was returned to family four days later. Against family's wishes, he was declared a 'martyr' and funeral announcements were published without family permission. Family emphasized he was neither a Basiji nor a member of IRGC. He was an athlete and bodybuilder. Funeral was held under heavy surveillance by plainclothes forces, with family facing restrictions, pressure and threats.
Shot three times in Azadegan area of Karaj, died as a martyr for the homeland
Died in Qarchak
Shot by direct fire from Islamic Republic suppressive forces. The bullet hit his face causing fatal brain damage.
17-year-old teenager killed by suppressive agents of the Islamic Republic
Died in Heravi area of Tehran. Also known as Mahsa Nabati
Killed on Friday, resident of Mahdasht Karaj, had two daughters
Died on 18 Dey 1404 in Karaj
Shot directly by regime suppression forces during protests in Meymand, Fars Province
Shot in the head with war bullets by suppressors in Meymand, Fars
Shot by Islamic Republic agents during protests in Nasim Shahr

Worked at a pharmacy. Was present at protests with his friend. First his friend was shot and killed on the spot, then Ramtin was killed by a direct shot to the head while trying to lift his friend from the ground.
17-year-old killed in bloody Dey month by regime forces in Vavan, Islamshahr. Described as a hero of the homeland whose name was not documented anywhere
Shot directly in the head around Kakh Square in Gorgan. He was the breadwinner of his family, having previously lost his father and brother, and was the only support his mother had.
Shot in the head by direct gunfire from suppressive forces during popular protests at the fifth roundabout in Fardis. Father of a young daughter.
Described as a child of Iran who sacrificed his life for the homeland
29-year-old pharmaceutical chemistry graduate working at a pharmacy, killed by regime mercenaries on Thursday in Chaloos

Shot in the head by Islamic Republic agents in Kakh Square in Gorgan during a gathering. Before attending the gathering, he had written on his Instagram: 'If one day I am not here, know that I went for the homeland, for freedom.'
Shot and killed in Dezful. Body was buried at midnight on January 12, 2026 under heavy security conditions.

19-year-old law student who was shot with pellet bullets in his leg and back on Thursday January 8th, but despite his injuries joined protesters again on Friday January 9th and was killed by war bullets at Qazi intersection in Dezful

Shot directly by Islamic Republic agents on Simindastgerd Three-Way Street while attempting to install a flag. He was first threatened with laser targeting, then shot directly in the head after the area became empty. His body remained in custody of security agencies for one week and was returned to his family after payment of a heavy sum. Witnesses reported that anyone attempting to approach his body was beaten or threatened, forcing his friends to leave the scene. His burial ceremony was attended by large crowds despite security threats to prevent public gathering.

Killed on January 8, 2026 (18 Dey) in Tehran. His body was handed over to the family after one week. He was buried in his hometown Masjed Soleyman at 4 AM in the presence of five male family members and several security forces. He was married and had one daughter.

Kurdish citizen from Kermanshah, 41 years old and father of a young child, taxi driver, killed by gunfire from military-security forces of the Islamic Republic during popular protests in Alvand city, Qazvin. His body was buried on 21 Dey in 'Behesht Fatemieh' Alvand.
A resident of Kermanshah, killed during protests in Karaj after being shot by Iranian government forces.
Killed by gunfire from military-security forces of the Islamic Republic during popular protests in Qarchak Varamin, Tehran Province. Originally from Sonqor
19-year-old student from Esfarayen in North Khorasan province, killed during suppression by Islamic Republic forces in Dey month 1404
Died in Khorramabad on January 8, 2026. Listed as victim #2395 in memorial account documenting protest casualties.
Death occurred in Tehran on 18 Dey 1404

42-year-old architect and artist, born in Arak and resident of Andisheh town in Fardis, Karaj. Shot by plainclothes agents of the Islamic Republic with a pistol without the sound of gunfire being heard, from very close range. His body was found in Kahrizak after 4 days of searching. Those close to him said that instead of saying goodbye during meetings, he would say: 'In hope of freedom'.
Shot and killed in Karaj city. According to reports, the shooting was carried out from the roof of a police station and he was killed as a result of being hit by a bullet.

Athlete and bodybuilder, shot with a war bullet to his lung during protests in Fooladshahr, Isfahan. His body was found a week later in Baghe Rezvan cemetery in Isfahan after family had no information about his whereabouts, and was buried two days later in his birthplace. His parents are suffering and he had recently bought a Peugeot 405 car with difficulty but the ownership documents had not yet been transferred to his name, having purchased it only two months prior.

17-year-old Kurdish student, originally from Salas-e Babajani in Kermanshah province and a resident of Tehran, shot directly by Iranian government forces during popular protests in the capital. He was a member of the Tehran Youth Premier Football League and a youth football player with Saipa Club. He was also a swimming champion in his age category and active in other sports including wrestling. Family received confirmation of his death on Monday after four days without information about his whereabouts. Family has not yet been allowed to take possession of his body.

17-year-old youth player for Saipa FC and member of Tehran's youth league, killed by direct fire from Islamic Republic forces during Iran's national uprising. Swimming champion and multi-sport athlete. Family learned of his death on Tuesday, January 13th after four days with no information. No official details released about exact time and place of death.

11-year-old child who went to the street with his father on January 8th in Neyshabur and was killed by Islamic Republic security forces. Security forces buried his small body at night and did not allow the family to be present at his burial. The family was prevented from any planning, notification, or holding ceremonies and the family is still under severe pursuit, surveillance and threats.

17-year-old volleyball player and member of Setaregan Alborz team in Karaj. Shot directly by Islamic Republic suppression forces in the lung area during protests at Mosbah intersection in Karaj. He was with his family during protests but got separated in the crowd and disappeared. His body was found hours later at a clinic. Family was forced to pay heavy costs to receive his body and buried him under security restrictions in Hajiabad cemetery in Karaj.

Injured by government forces during protests at Esteghlal Square in Parand, Tehran on Thursday January 9, 2026. Family found her three days later in the intensive care unit of Fatemeh Zahra Hospital in Robat Karim. She died on January 28, 2026, unable to endure her injuries.

22-year-old Iranian daughter killed by suppressive forces of the regime during Iran's national uprising in Qazvin. She was shot and killed simply for standing with the people and demanding life and a future.

Lor man, married father of four, killed by direct live fire from Iranian government forces during public protests in Fuladshahr, Isfahan Province. Two of his brothers were also severely injured during the same protests and are currently hospitalized, with one in a coma.

Shot with war bullets during protests at 'Seh-rah Simin' (Simin Crossroads) in Isfahan, hit in the shoulder area. Described as tall, very brave and kind, was at the front of the crowd when he was hit by three war bullets, with the shoulder bullet reaching his heart and causing his death.
Died in Malayer on 18 Dey 1404
Shot directly by Islamic Republic suppression forces in Afsarieh neighborhood of Tehran

Shot with direct gunfire on the night of the 18th in the city of Dansfahan (one of the counties of Qazvin Province with 14,000 population). Had been fighting against the Islamic Republic for a long time. When he was killed, authorities demanded 200 million tomans to release his body and buried him at 5 AM in a heavily secured environment without notice or presence of anyone except his family. The family was not even allowed to print obituaries or hold mourning ceremonies.
Commemorative post by his father after his death. Father said: 'My dear son, with your departure you gave us honor but broke my back. But [you are] sacrificed for dear Iran.'
Shot with 2 bullets on Lashgar street in Gorgan
Died in Eslamshahr on 18 Dey 1404
Killed by direct shot to the head in Ariashahr, Tehran

Shot with seven bullets by Islamic Republic suppression forces. His burial ceremony was held under the strictest security measures with only ten family members allowed to attend. The transfer and burial of his body was conducted with armed escort by security agents and the area around the burial site was completely under government forces control.

34-year-old protester who disappeared after participating in protests on 18 Dey in Shahin Shahr, Isfahan Province. His body was found days later in a morgue at one of the cemeteries with bullet wounds. This silent murder is part of the organized killing of protesters in Iran's national uprising.

Killed on 18 Dey in Zanjan. Father of a 5-year-old daughter. Buried at 7 AM in the presence of security forces and plainclothes agents of the Islamic Republic. No news coverage of his death. Heavy security atmosphere in Zanjan with many killed but families under severe control.

Shot directly in the head by Islamic Republic suppression forces in front of the police station on Sabzeh Meydan Street in Zanjan. Father of a 5-year-old daughter. Relative of Mehrdad Ghorbani who was killed in the 401 (referring to 1401/2022 protests). Family was under pressure and did not allow any ceremony to be held, and the body was buried at 5 AM.
Shot by Islamic Republic agents in Zanjan. He was the father of a 4-year-old daughter.

27-year-old young man killed during protests in Eslam Abad-e Gharb with direct shooting by government suppressive forces. Near Imam Hussein mosque, first severely wounded with shotgun pellets, then government forces shot him in the heart with Kalashnikov rifle while he was lying on the ground. Buried on January 10 in Tajar village, Karam Panahabad

Born in Mehr 1367, 37 years old, resident of Marzdaran neighborhood in Tehran, owner of a cabinet-making workshop in Ariashahr. Shot dead by direct fire from suppression forces during protests in Ariashahr district. Was with five friends at protests when one was shot. Returned to help his wounded friend when a sniper affiliated with security forces fired from a building rooftop. Security forces then targeted him with six war bullets, killing him on the spot. About to get married, gave his phone, password and car keys to his mother before leaving home and asked for her forgiveness.
Death occurred in Mashhad
Killed by Islamic Republic agents on 18 Dey 1404. Originally from Mazandaran and buried in Chalrud village in Noor township.
Killed by suppressive forces on Kish island. Body was found full of wounds and bruises, riddled with bullets. Described as being killed mercilessly by government forces.

Shot in the throat by suppressors from Kamil Region 2 in Torogh neighborhood of Mashhad. He had gone to save his wounded friend and was also wounded. Both died together. He was a newly married man, only one year after his wedding.
Died on 18 Dey 1404 in Mashhad during the protests

Went to Haft Tir area to try to save Amir Bughi when he learned that Amir had gone there. He understood what freedom meant and tried to help. He was killed, likely during the same incident as Amir Bughi.
Ten-year flower seller in Shiraz, known as a calm and gentle person whom the entire neighborhood would swear by his name. Shot directly in the face on Thursday 19 Dey in Shiraz.

Shot with war ammunition in the lung area during protests. Originally from Shabad, lived in various areas including Shahryarshahr and Vali-e Asr Shabad before moving with family to Ni-pehan village in Qasr-e Shirin due to his father's agricultural lands. Was a tattoo artist who moved to Karaj with his wife for career advancement. Body was delivered to family after extensive pressure and difficulty - many victims' bodies were reportedly stacked on top of each other in a warehouse. Burial was forced to be conducted quietly at 10 AM instead of the planned 2:30 PM ceremony due to security pressure. Buried under heavy security presence with IRGC forces, plainclothes agents, mobile phone and internet cuts, with only close relatives present. Family continues to face unspecified security pressures.
Died on January 8, 2026 in Eslamshahr (note: source mentions no image is available for this immortalized name)
Died in Fardis, Karaj
Retired teacher who was shot four times in the chest and killed while attempting to help a young girl who had been wounded in the head

Retired teacher killed during popular protests when government suppression forces fired five war bullets directly at his leg and heart. He was trying to help a female protester who had fallen to the ground after being shot in the head by suppression forces. He was first shot in the leg, then killed with four additional bullets to his heart.

16-year-old child from Qochan working as a laborer, killed during popular protests in Karaj when shot in the heart by a military bullet fired by Islamic Republic military-security forces. Family was forced to pay 750 million tomans to receive his body. Burial ceremony held in Qochan.
Shot and killed in Chaharmahal and Bakhtiari. His family faced repeated threats while trying to recover his body
Shot in the heart by government forces. From Esfarayen. Described as sacrificing his life for the homeland.

25-year-old from Gerash, Fars who worked in Bandar Abbas. Killed by government forces during peaceful protests in Bandar Abbas on 18 Dey. His body was returned to his family four days later under security conditions to be buried in silence.
Death occurred in Javabad, Isfahan
Death occurred in Karaj on 18 Dey 1404

Father of a young daughter under two years old. Shot in the leg near Sabzeh Meydan area of Rasht around 8 PM. After being shot, he was transferred from hospital while still connected to IV and blood transfusion. According to an eyewitness, his body was found the next day in a container in Bagh-e Rezvan cemetery in Rasht with extensive blood traces at the scene. Had grown up without a father since childhood.
Died in Tarom on January 8, 2026 (18 Dey 1404). Listed as entry #2288 in a memorial list.
Kurdish citizen shot by Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps forces during popular protests in Karnachi neighborhood of Kermanshah

Shot during suppression of nationwide protests in the evening of 18 Dey in Afsariyeh neighborhood of Tehran, on 29th Street in front of his business place, targeted with direct bullet shot from behind at close range. His body was delivered to his family with delay and his burial ceremony was held on 25 Dey. He had recently married and was planning to build a new future with his wife at the beginning of their married life.

20-year-old Kurdish citizen from Sirvan, Ilam province, killed by direct gunfire from Islamic Republic suppression forces during popular protests in Tehran on Thursday evening 18 Dey 1404. Shot with war bullets.

From Sirvan County in Ilam Province and a resident of Tehran, killed during protests on 17 Shahrivar Street near Khorasan Square after being shot by Iranian government forces. His body was returned to the family and buried.
Killed during the Dey 1404 (January 2026) protests in Islamshahr, Tehran province
Cinema actor and assistant director from Bandar Kiashahr in Gilan province, killed during protests in Rasht by bullet impact from Islamic Republic of Iran government forces

22-year-old student in Nurabad Mamasani. Shot by Islamic Republic agents on 23 Dey 1404. He and his friend Aryan Keshkoli were targeted in a shooting. After Aryan was shot and killed while in a moving vehicle, the car crashed into a concrete block in the middle of the road. Islamic Republic agents then shot Reza as he was fleeing after exiting the vehicle.

22-year-old monarchist from Izeh, killed at 10 PM on Hafez South Street in Izeh by security forces with three bullets. His family broke into the morgue at night to retrieve his body to prevent the Islamic Republic from conducting forced interviews and receiving money from them. He was buried at 5 AM on 19 Dey 1404 in Takht Kashan village in Izeh.

Lur citizen from Ramhormoz city in Khuzestan Province, resident of Karaj. Killed by direct gunfire from government suppressive forces in Karaj. Shot directly with war weapon, bullet hit his head area. The severity of injuries caused his head to be completely destroyed. After his father's death, he had migrated from Ramhormoz to Karaj to support his family, working as a barber and salesman to cover living expenses for himself and his mother. He was among children who were forced to work from a young age.
32 years old, from Ramhormoz, Khuzestan, of Lor ethnicity. Shot with military weapons by suppressive forces in Fardis, Karaj.
Killed by Seyyed Ali Khamenei, described as an innate criminal, in Sirjan. Called a child of Iran and one who sacrificed his life for the homeland.
Shot by suppression forces in Yazdanshahr, Isfahan

Shot by Islamic Republic agents in Fooladshahr, Isfahan. Hit by 47 pellets, then arrested by agents. His lifeless body was returned to family after several days with broken hands, nose and teeth, and marks of injuries from electric shock device. Buried in Farrokhshahr, Chaharmahal and Bakhtiari province. Worked at Snapp and MDF industry.

Described as a teenager hero who sacrificed his life for freedom in Sabz Square in Zanjan in front of the police station with two bullets, became a martyr for the homeland. His shoes were left behind on the street.
17-year-old only child of his family and student in Zanjan. Shot by Khamenei's forces with bullets that hit his head and back on the night of 18 Dey

Kurdish woman from Marivan studying textile and fashion design at Shariati College in Tehran. Shot in the head from close range from behind after joining a protest after leaving college. Family traveled from Kermanshah to Tehran to identify her body among hundreds of young people. Intelligence forces surrounded family home and prevented burial, forcing family to bury her body along the road between Kermanshah and Kamyaran.
Shot and killed by suppressive forces on Eqbal Street in Kerman. He was killed minutes after his son Amirali was shot in front of the family shop.

45-year-old protester, married and father of two children. Joined protests in Mashhad with his friends on 18 Dey 1404. Went missing during clashes between protesters and security forces in the '7 Tir' area of Mashhad. After 5 days missing, his family found his body in 'Behesht Reza' cemetery in Mashhad, with signs of beating on his body and a bullet in his side.
Died in Shahriar on 18 Dey 1404
Killed by the Islamic Republic regime

Mechanical engineer and graduate of Gilan University who ran a classic car repair shop. Shot in the side during popular protests in Sadeghiyeh area of Tehran while present on the street with her sister. Her sister transported her bloodied body by motorcycle toward the hospital, but she died before reaching the medical center. Family was subjected to severe security pressures to refrain from publicizing news of her death.

Recently graduated student from Amirkabir University of Technology (Tehran Polytechnic), class of 2019. Killed during the massacre on 18th and 19th Dey month in Tehran. Her body was recently returned to her family, and due to pressure, there has been limited information dissemination about this martyr. Initial information about her death was first published through stories and messages from friends and classmates on social networks.

Mechanic who was 24 years old, described as a happy and cheerful young man. He had just bought equipment and wanted to rent a shop to work independently. Shot with one bullet to his side and one bullet to his head in Chalous. His body was returned with threats and commitments. During his funeral procession, people chanted 'Death to Khamenei'.
Shot in the head with a war bullet by Khamenei's agents in Shahsavar. He was from Neshtarud in Shahsavar (Tonekabon), Mazandaran province.

Kurdish citizen from Lahijan, Gilan province. Shot by suppressive forces during people's protests. Targeted by a sniper from the top of the police station building, with the bullet hitting below his eye.
Died on 18 Dey 1404 in Eslamshahr
Died on January 8, 2026 in Eslamshahr
Shot directly with war bullets by IRGC (Revolutionary Guards) in Lahijan, body was buried at midnight without permission for mourning ceremony
Victim documented in Iran International Memorial database from Homayounshahr, Isfahan
Shot directly in the heart during protests in Azimiyeh area of Karaj. Originally from Hashtrud (Saraskand) in East Azerbaijan Province, residing in Karaj. Son of Mohammad Hussein.

Shop owner who went out toward Khaqani street in Isfahan on Thursday night. Shot directly in the leg, arrested while wounded, then executed with a shot to the head while in custody. Body returned after one week for 400 million rials
Shot with direct fire by Iranian regime forces in Tehran. He was a citizen of Talesh from Masal and resident of Qods city in Tehran. He had recently married.

Shot in the stomach in Tehran. When they saw he was still alive, they suffocated him. His body was thrown into an empty lot in Tehran that was full of corpses. Family searched everywhere for him, then authorities told them to go find and take their child's body from there. The family retrieved the body with great difficulty and buried him in their village near Quchan.
Cousin of Ilya Qodsi, fruit seller and father of two, shot in the back of the head by Islamic Republic forces during protests in the same attack
Killed during 2025-2026 Iranian protests

Graduate architecture student shot by government forces during nighttime shooting on Mehranpour Street in Narmak, Tehran. He was with his family members when the shooting occurred and was trying to move them away from the incident location when he was targeted by bullets from Islamic Republic regime agents. He was shot in the kidney area, transferred to Ansari Hospital in Narmak, but died despite medical efforts.
38-year-old from Azimiyeh, Karaj killed on 18 or 19 Dey 1404
Shot on 18 Dey. Was missing for three days after being shot, and news of him was obtained in Kahrizak. A heavy sum was received from his family for the return of his body.
Shot directly in the neck with a bullet in Phase One Andisheh area of Karaj
Shot with shotgun pellets to the head in front of Police Station 11 in Shahroud on Thursday 18 Dey
Shot in the throat by regime mercenaries from behind in Farmandarei Square in Rafsanjan. After being transferred to hospital, his last words were 'Tell mom I love her'
30 years old, married, national and Asian Muay Thai champion, shot directly with bullet from behind in front of his wife and mother by government agents during popular protests

Engineer at Arak Refinery who disappeared on January 8, 2026 during protests on Shariati Street in Arak. His family had no information about his whereabouts for 6 days until his lifeless body was handed over to them at the Arak morgue on January 14, 2026. Authorities provided no clear explanation regarding the circumstances of his death or whether he had been detained prior to his killing.

Shot in the head by security forces while bending down to pick up a stone on the main street of Kamalshahr. He was with a group of young people from the neighborhood when he became the target of direct gunfire and was hit in the head. His body was held at Behesht Sakineh cemetery for about a week, and its release was conditional on payment of money and transferring the burial location outside the family's main residence area. The family refused the request to transfer the body to another city, and after about eight days, the body was handed over with the condition of burial in a small cemetery around Karaj. Despite the proximity of Behesht Sakineh cemetery to the family's residence, they were not allowed to bury him there.
Killed during protests in Azimiyeh, Karaj area. Date uncertain - either 18 or 19 Dey 1404

He was a bazaar merchant and father of a seven-year-old son. He had previously been detained for two months in 1401.

Shot directly in the heart during a chase with security forces in Masoor neighborhood of Khorramabad. Family immediately washed and shrouded his body to prevent security forces from taking it. Security forces later came to the family home attempting to forcibly take the body, but family elders resisted and after a confrontation, the agents were forced to retreat and flee

Kurdish citizen from Qoch Tepe village in Asadabad, Hamedan, father of two children. Shot in the leg by Islamic Republic military-security forces during popular protests in Saveh city in Markazi province on 18 Dey 1404. After being transferred to a hospital in Tehran, he died two days later due to severity of injuries. Security institutions did not allow transfer of his body to his hometown and family was forced to bury him in Saveh city.

Shot in the head with live ammunition by government forces during popular protests. Killed by a sniper positioned on top of a building near the gathering location. He had made a will requesting that if killed by Islamic Republic forces, his death announcement should state he was a 'martyr for the homeland', but security forces prevented his family from honoring this request and called him a 'rioter' instead. Originally from Ashtian in Markazi Province.
Father of two sons. Killed by Islamic Republic agents at the third roundabout of Fardis, Karaj
17-year-old Arab youth from Abadan's Maniohi district, killed by direct gunfire from suppression forces while participating in nationwide protests. He had traveled to Tehran for construction work.
Killed by Islamic Republic suppressors during protests in Arak. His family had no news of him for five days until they found his lifeless body at the Arak cemetery morgue.
Killed by Khamenei's agents on 18 Dey. Described as a hero of the nation and another brave person from Rasht.

Shot with pellet gun targeting his entire body in Nazi Abad, then Islamic Republic suppression forces approached him and struck his face and head multiple times with batons, causing brain injury and his death

Civil engineer and student at Azad University of Hashtgerd. Shot and killed by Islamic Republic agents with two bullets - first bullet fired from inside Gohardasht mosque (opposite the main Gohardasht police station) hit his side, second bullet hit the jugular artery in his neck. Died on the spot in front of his wife's eyes. Had participated continuously in protests from 2017 to 2025. Family was religious and regime-supporting. Body was initially not released, family had to pay costs related to bullets before burial was allowed three days later at local Atishgah cemetery with security forces present.
26 years old, from Islamabad-e Gharb, died in Kermanshah on Friday. Described as a 'sacrificer on the path of freedom' who 'sacrificed his precious life to the homeland'

Teacher who was found beheaded. Body was discovered unidentified in Razvan Garden. Family spent one week looking for him. Authorities delayed burial for three weeks under the pretext of identity verification.

Former member of national youth taekwondo team and physical education student. Shot in the head with a bullet in the Razavi Square area of Rasht. According to sources, he had told his companions that day 'I'm going to help people.' Sources say agents pursued him and he was directly shot in the head in a dead-end alley. His body was initially transferred to Rasoul Akram Hospital in Rasht and then to forensic medicine on Friday. Family was asked to pay money to receive the body, which they refused. They were also given a paper to declare that Shayan was a 'Basiji', which the family again rejected. Only parents were allowed to receive the body on Tuesday for burial, with family pledging not to hold any ceremonies. Two days before the 40th day memorial, individuals came to the family home saying 'we didn't know Shayan was a champion' and requested to hold the memorial ceremony themselves. During burial, the taekwondo federation honored his mother with a plaque of appreciation. He had won a silver medal at the Asian Club Cup.
Shot from behind with a war bullet by Islamic Republic forces during protests in Gohardasht area. He was a 25-year-old unmarried man who came to the streets for freedom and human dignity.

Student at Beheshti National University, daughter of Fatah and Shirin, from Baneh city, killed by direct gunfire from suppressive forces during popular protests in Tehran. Shot directly in the head with two war bullets by Islamic Republic suppression forces in Fardis, Karaj. She had a one-year-old son and was buried in Behesht-e Ali cemetery in Karaj.

Shot with live ammunition while dancing Kurdish dances around a fire in Punak Square after a placard of Qassem Soleimani was burned. Shot with a bullet to his back by Islamic Republic forces. He was 37 years old, had recently gotten his life together, renovated his house, and was planning to marry his partner. Despite his mother's insistence that he not go, he said 'Mother, if I don't go, who will? Look at our living situation, you take care of yourselves, I'll watch out for myself.' He went out and never returned.

22-year-old from Kermanshah who died on the 8th of Dey month 1404 as a result of baton strikes to the head. His mother announced on Man o To network that due to government pressures, months have passed and his name has not been mentioned, and she just wants us to be the voice of dear Sina.

Shot with direct gunfire by suppressive forces. Had family relationship with Matin Montazerzohour. Both were concerned about freedom and went to the streets, but the response to their protest against the current situation was bullets.

25-year-old owner of a flower shop named 'Shahede' in Gorgan, shot and killed by security forces during protests on 18 Dey 1404. His body was buried at Imamzadeh Abdullah in Javadiyeh neighborhood of Gorgan.

After about 10 hours, no clear information was given to the family, and the next day bodies were transferred from the hospital by security agencies. The family went through a long, ambiguous process with pressure and threats to receive the body. After about 10 days, the family was told the body could only be buried with limited attendance and no public ceremony. Younes had been working since adolescence in jobs like construction and welding, and just one week before his death had started a new job as a CNC cutting machine operator at a company.
